outerrim_cobble.name: Cobble
outerrim_cobble.description: Outer Rim starter vessel. Mismatched parts, personally tuned engine, expired warranty.
outerrim_cobble.lore:
No two Cobbles are alike because no two Cobbles are made from the same
parts. Assembled from whatever was available — Solarian hull panels here,
Crimson thruster housing there, something Voidborn that nobody can identify
bolted to the port nacelle. The engine has been hand-tuned by whoever built
this particular Cobble, and it is improbably, irresponsibly fast. The hull
integrity is... present. The warranty expired before the ship was finished.
The paint doesn't match. The instrument panel is labeled in three different
languages. It is the most honest ship in the galaxy: exactly what it is,
nothing pretending to be more. Rim pilots love them with an affection that
baffles every other empire.

nebula_prospect.name: Prospect
nebula_prospect.description: Nebula starter vessel. Pre-loaded price database. Best cargo in its class.
nebula_prospect.lore:
The Prospect comes with the Federation's compliments — and a pre-loaded
database of current commodity prices across 200 stations, a loyalty chip
tied to your Federation trade account, and a glossy brochure outlining
upgrade paths starting at just 1,999 credits. The expanded cargo hold
isn't generosity; it's an investment in future transaction volume. The
fuel-efficient drive was a practical decision — every credit spent on fuel
is a credit not spent at a Federation marketplace. Includes a 90-day
trial of Federation Premium Trade Intelligence (auto-renews at
competitive rates).

crimson_shard.name: Shard
crimson_shard.description: Crimson starter vessel. Repurposed combat drone. Mining laser doubles as weapon.
crimson_shard.lore:
The Shard was never designed as a mining vessel. It was a combat drone —
autonomous, disposable, built to absorb fire while delivering ordnance.
When the Crimson Fleet needed a starter ship, their engineers did what
Crimson engineers always do: they bolted a mining laser where the torpedo
tube used to be and called it done. The extra weapon slot is original
equipment. The reduced cargo bay is a consequence of the armor plating
that no one bothered to remove. Crimson pilots wouldn't have it any other
way — even ore hauling should be done from a position of strength.

solarian_theoria.name: Theoria
solarian_theoria.description: Solarian starter mining vessel. Certified, calibrated, warranty-covered.
solarian_theoria.lore:
Every Solarian pilot's journey begins with a Theoria. Named for the ancient
Greek concept of contemplative knowledge, this vessel embodies the Confederacy's
belief that understanding precedes action. The 300-page operations manual
(mandatory reading, examination required) covers everything from optimal ore
extraction angles to emergency hull breach poetry recitation protocols.
Over-engineered by three separate review committees, the Theoria has more
diagnostic systems than most ships have systems. The extended utility bus
accommodates the Confederacy's insistence that every vessel carry redundant
sensor packages. Comes with a two-year comprehensive warranty that covers
everything except "pilot-initiated misadventure."

voidborn_threshold.name: Threshold
voidborn_threshold.description: Voidborn starter vessel. Grown, not built. Anticipates pilot intent.
voidborn_threshold.lore:
The Threshold is not manufactured. It is cultivated in Nexus Prime's orbital
growth bays over a period the Collective insists on calling "gestation." The
hull is a crystalline-organic composite that repairs microscopic fractures
autonomously. The shield generators draw power from sources the engineering
manual describes only as "adjacent." New pilots report that the ship
anticipates course corrections before they're input — the Collective
considers this a feature, not a concern. The cabin temperature is always
slightly wrong, regardless of thermostat settings. No Voidborn engineer
has ever acknowledged this as a defect.

outerrim_prayer.name: Prayer
outerrim_prayer.description: Cargo containers welded to an engine. Pilot straps in outside. No shields, no weapons, no excuses.
outerrim_prayer.lore:
The Prayer-class is not a ship. It is cargo containers welded to an
engine with a seat bolted to the outside. There is no cockpit. There
is no life support. There is no shield generator, no weapon mount, no
defense system, and no module slot of any kind. The pilot wears a
spacesuit and holds on. Four hundred units of cargo capacity at tier
one — more than most ships three tiers higher. The catch is everything
else: 40 hull means a stiff breeze is lethal, and there is literally
nothing you can do to improve the ship's survivability. It cannot be
upgraded. It cannot be armed. It can only carry things from one place
to another and hope nothing goes wrong along the way. Named for what
the pilot does before undocking.

outerrim_loose_change.name: Loose Change
outerrim_loose_change.description: Outer Rim shuttle. Cheap, fast, seats six uncomfortably.
outerrim_loose_change.lore:
The Loose Change costs about as much as the fuel to fly it. Outer Rim
stations keep these stacked in hangars like disposable cups — grab one,
fly somewhere, leave it for the next person. The seats were salvaged from
six different vehicle types and none of them match. The environmental
controls have two settings: "too cold" and "broken." But it's fast, it's
cheap, and it gets you where you're going. Eventually.

outerrim_rubble.name: Rubble
outerrim_rubble.description: Outer Rim mining platform. Several previous owners. Currently operational. No questions about the welds.
outerrim_rubble.lore:
The Rubble-class is what the Patchwork builds when it doesn't have the
components to build anything better. The mining laser is original
equipment. The hull is a composite of three different ship frames that
happened to fit. The cargo hold has a slight lean to port that the
previous owner described as "character." It mines ore. That is the
complete list of things it does. For most Rim pilots, that's enough.

nebula_floor_price.name: Floor Price
nebula_floor_price.description: Nebula entry-level freighter. For pilots who find the standard options too expensive. They are correct.
nebula_floor_price.lore:
The Federation offers the Floor Price to pilots who point at the
standard hauler catalog and say "cheaper." The cargo hold is 60%
of a Dividend at 70% of the price, which the marketing department
considers an attractive value proposition. The defensive systems
consist of hull plating and optimism. No automated routing, no
cargo manifest system, no crew comfort features — the pilot gets
a seat, a throttle, and enough hold space to make the run
worthwhile. The name was chosen by the accounting department.

outerrim_finders_keepers.name: Finders Keepers
outerrim_finders_keepers.description: Outer Rim salvage platform. First to the wreck wins. Everything else is negotiable.
outerrim_finders_keepers.lore:
The Finders Keepers is the Rim's entry-level salvage platform, built on
the principle that the first ship to reach a wreck gets the best picks.
The oversized engine relative to hull mass means it arrives at debris
fields before heavier competitors, and the cargo hold — repurposed from
a Nebula shipping container that fell off a freighter — holds enough to
make the trip worthwhile. Rim salvage law is three words long: finders
keepers, losers weepers. The Finders Keepers is named accordingly.

outerrim_scrapheap.name: Scrapheap
outerrim_scrapheap.description: Outer Rim mining vessel. Ugly but effective. Practically indestructible through redundancy.
outerrim_scrapheap.lore:
The Scrapheap looks like someone welded three different ships together
and added a mining laser. That's because that's exactly what happened.
Rim engineers discovered that by combining redundant systems from
multiple wrecks, you get a ship where nothing works perfectly but
everything has a backup. When the primary power coupling fails, the
secondary (from a Solarian shuttle) takes over. When that fails, the
tertiary (from gods-know-what) kicks in. It's not elegant. It works.

outerrim_claim_jumper.name: Claim Jumper
outerrim_claim_jumper.description: Outer Rim strip miner. Fast, fragile, grabs what it can before someone notices.
outerrim_claim_jumper.lore:
Named for a time-honored Rim tradition, the Claim Jumper is built for
speed-mining: arrive at an asteroid belt before anyone else, extract
everything worth taking, and leave before the legal owner shows up.
The oversized engines are from a decommissioned racer, which explains
both the speed and the alarming vibration at full thrust. No weapons
because weapons add mass, and mass slows you down, and slow miners get
caught. Rim mining philosophy in hull form: take what you can, leave
what you can't carry, run if you have to.

crimson_levy.name: Levy
crimson_levy.description: Crimson Pact recruit-grade fighter. Two weapons, no electronics, built to be expendable. You earn better hardware.
crimson_levy.lore:
New Pact recruits don't get the good hardware. They get the Levy. Two
cannons, steel plate, and a hull that's been tested to survive exactly
as long as necessary. No targeting computer — the Fleet considers this
a training feature. No sensor suite — learn to use your eyes. The Levy
is mass-produced in bulk at War Citadel from materials that don't
require the supply chains the better ships depend on. If you come back
from three sorties in a Levy, you've earned an upgrade. Most do.

solarian_principia.name: Principia
solarian_principia.description: Solarian research shuttle. Extra CPU for scientific instruments.
solarian_principia.lore:
Named after Newton's masterwork, the Principia is designed for those who
believe the universe reveals its secrets to patient observers with good
instruments. Carries no weapons by default — the Confederacy considers it
gauche to arm a research vessel, though the utility slots can accommodate
defensive measures for "field researchers in contested territories."

crimson_shiv.name: Shiv
crimson_shiv.description: Disposable Crimson boarding craft. Gets marines to the target hull. Everything after that is their problem.
crimson_shiv.lore:
The Shiv has no weapons because it IS the weapon. Twelve tons of
tungsten-reinforced prow designed to breach hull plating and deliver
a boarding party directly into the enemy ship's interior. The design
is deliberately cheap — Crimson Fleet accountants classify Shivs as
ammunition, not vessels. The armor rating is excellent for its size
because the entire ship is essentially armor wrapped around a cockpit
and a marine compartment. No weapons, minimal fuel, maximum speed
toward the target. Shiv pilots either become heroes or statistics,
and the Crimson Fleet honors both.

outerrim_technically_legal.name: Technically Legal
outerrim_technically_legal.description: Outer Rim courier. Modified beyond manufacturer specs. Somehow still flies.
outerrim_technically_legal.lore:
Every component in a Technically Legal has been modified, bypassed, or
replaced with something that works "well enough." The engine governor was
removed "for maintenance" and never reinstalled. The fuel injectors are
from a ship two size classes larger. The safety interlocks have been
recalibrated to more "frontier-appropriate" thresholds. It passes
inspection because the inspection standards haven't caught up with the
modifications. Yet.

nebula_tender.name: Tender
nebula_tender.description: Nebula shuttle. Efficient personnel and light cargo transfers.
nebula_tender.lore:
The Tender-class is the Federation's workhorse for station-to-station
transfers, VIP pickups, and light cargo runs that don't justify spinning
up a freighter. Cheap to operate, cheap to insure, and cheap to replace
— three qualities the Federation considers virtues. The comfortable
passenger cabin is a courtesy that the Federation bills separately.

solarian_datum.name: Datum
solarian_datum.description: Solarian minimum-viable survey vessel. Rigorously tested to be just enough. No redundant systems.
solarian_datum.lore:
The Datum is the Confederacy's minimum viable survey platform,
defined after three committee reviews and a memo from the materials
science division on circuit availability. No sensor array — pilots
source their own. No defensive weapons — the Confederacy considers
retreat a valid tactical option. Fuel range was classified
non-negotiable from the first meeting, and that remained the only
point of unanimous agreement.

crimson_buckler.name: Buckler
crimson_buckler.description: Cheap Crimson escort. Bodyguards convoys. More defense than offense, for once.
crimson_buckler.lore:
The Buckler is the Crimson Fleet's only ship where defense slots
outnumber weapon slots, and the engineers responsible for the design
reportedly received several pointed questions about their loyalty.
The rationale survived scrutiny: even the Crimson Fleet needs convoy
escorts, and a dead escort protects nothing. Two defense slots allow
layered shielding that keeps the Buckler between the cargo and the
threat long enough for the actual warships to arrive. Buckler pilots
are respected for their willingness to absorb fire — a form of
aggression the Fleet grudgingly acknowledges.

solarian_cogito.name: Cogito
solarian_cogito.description: Solarian courier. Fast, fuel-efficient, minimal but reliable.
solarian_cogito.lore:
"I think, therefore I deliver on time." The Cogito sacrifices cargo space
and armament for speed and fuel efficiency. Popular among Solarian
diplomatic couriers and express mail services. The onboard AI is
certified polite in seven languages and will not judge the contents
of your packages. Much.

crimson_crowbar.name: Crowbar
crimson_crowbar.description: Crimson salvage tug. Reclaims battlefield wreckage — and discourages competition from reclaiming it first.
crimson_crowbar.lore:
The Crowbar-class exists because the Crimson Fleet produces more wrecks
than it can process. Two weapon mounts on a salvage tug raised eyebrows
at other empires' shipyards. The Pact considers them standard equipment
— wreck fields attract scavengers, and scavengers attract the Crowbar's
other function. Fleet salvage doctrine is simple: if something is in the
wreck field and you did not put it there, it is a hostile.

nebula_dividend.name: Dividend
nebula_dividend.description: Nebula light hauler. Maximizes profit per trip.
nebula_dividend.lore:
The Dividend-class is the Federation's entry-level dedicated hauler,
designed around a single principle: maximize return on every fuel cell.
No weapons, because weapons take up space that could carry cargo, and
cargo generates revenue, and revenue is the whole point. The Federation's
marketing department calculates that a Dividend pays for itself in
approximately 40 round trips. They put this on the brochure.

voidborn_eigenstate.name: Eigenstate
voidborn_eigenstate.description: Voidborn sensor vessel. Perceives things other ships cannot.
voidborn_eigenstate.lore:
The Eigenstate-class carries sensor arrays that detect phenomena other
instruments classify as noise. Voidborn scouts have been known to navigate
around hazards that didn't appear on anyone else's charts until days later.
The ship carries no weapons — the Collective considers observation a
higher calling than destruction, though they acknowledge the two are
sometimes difficult to separate.

outerrim_frostbitten.name: Frostbitten
outerrim_frostbitten.description: Outer Rim ice scraper. Fast, cheap, held together with thermal tape and optimism.
outerrim_frostbitten.lore:
The Frostbitten-class got its name from what happens to the pilot's
extremities when the environmental controls fail — which they do,
regularly. The ice extraction system is an oversized saw blade and a
collection hopper, both salvaged from industrial equipment that was
never designed for zero-gravity operation. It works surprisingly well,
as long as you don't mind the vibration, the noise, and the occasional
chunk of ice that ricochets off the cockpit windshield. Rim ice miners
wear extra gloves. This is considered the complete safety protocol.

solarian_lemma.name: Lemma
solarian_lemma.description: Solarian scout. Fast, sensor-rich, built to observe and report.
solarian_lemma.lore:
In mathematics, a lemma is a proven statement used as a stepping stone to
a larger theorem. Likewise, the Lemma-class gathers the preliminary data
upon which all subsequent operations depend. The fastest ship in the
Solarian personal fleet, it sacrifices armament entirely in favor of
sensors, fuel, and the processing power to analyze findings in transit.

solarian_archimedes.name: Archimedes
solarian_archimedes.description: Solarian mining specialist. Enhanced ore processing and extra utility.
solarian_archimedes.lore:
"Give me a lever long enough and I shall move the world" — and give a Solarian
engineer a mining laser and they'll optimize it until it practically apologizes
for missing a seam. The Archimedes-class improves on the Theoria with a
dedicated ore processing subsystem and expanded cargo hold. Comes pre-loaded
with geological survey software covering 400+ known mineral compositions.

solarian_catalogue.name: Catalogue
solarian_catalogue.description: Solarian salvager. Wreck recovery as a data collection exercise — every fragment scanned and cross-referenced.
solarian_catalogue.lore:
The Catalogue-class approaches wreck recovery as a data collection exercise.
Every fragment is scanned, weighed, and cross-referenced against the
Confederacy's materials database before entering the cargo hold. Recovery
operations take longer than competitors', but the resulting inventory reports
are immaculate. Insurance adjusters across the sector prefer
Catalogue-generated wreck assessments — they're the only ones that come
pre-formatted.

outerrim_huffenpuff.name: Huff'n'Puff
outerrim_huffenpuff.description: Outer Rim gas scooper. Jury-rigged collection system on a racing frame.
outerrim_huffenpuff.lore:
The name is both a description of the collection process and what
happens to the pilot during a hull breach. Rim engineers welded gas
collection scoops to whatever hull was cheapest at the time, rerouted
the atmospheric intake, and called it an industrial vessel. The gas
collection system makes a noise that experienced pilots describe as
"concerning but profitable." Safety features include a fire
extinguisher duct-taped to the pilot's seat and a handwritten note
that says "DON'T SMOKE." The Rim's approach to volatile gas handling
is "be fast enough that the explosion is behind you."

voidborn_liminal.name: Liminal
voidborn_liminal.description: Voidborn mining specialist. Organic hull self-repairs while mining.
voidborn_liminal.lore:
The Liminal-class mining vessel incorporates more organic hull material
than most Voidborn ships, allowing it to metabolize trace minerals from
asteroid debris during extraction. Pilots report the cargo hold occasionally
rearranges itself for "better feng shui." The shield array provides
excellent protection during asteroid belt operations, where micro-impacts
that would degrade conventional hulls are simply absorbed.

nebula_margin.name: Margin
nebula_margin.description: Nebula courier. Fast delivery for time-sensitive contracts.
nebula_margin.lore:
Time is money, and the Margin-class is designed to convert fuel into
time as efficiently as possible. The Federation operates a galaxy-spanning
courier network, and the Margin is their workhorse — small enough to
dock anywhere, fast enough to meet deadlines, and carrying enough cargo
for premium express deliveries. The name refers to profit margins, which
the Federation assures you are excellent.

crimson_maul.name: Maul
crimson_maul.description: Armed Crimson mining vessel. Why stop mining just because someone's shooting at you?
crimson_maul.lore:
Crimson Fleet miners don't call for help. The Maul embodies this
principle with two weapon mounts on a mining frame — one for the
laser, one for whatever you need to point at whoever interrupted
your shift. The reinforced hull inherits from the Shard's combat
drone lineage. Some pilots mount two mining lasers and double their
yield. Others mount a mining laser and a cannon and dare anyone to
contest their asteroid claim. The Crimson Fleet considers both
approaches equally valid resource extraction strategies.

outerrim_no_refunds.name: No Refunds
outerrim_no_refunds.description: Outer Rim light fighter. Fastest in class. Structural warranty not included.
outerrim_no_refunds.lore:
The name is the sales pitch, the warranty, and the flight philosophy all
in one. The No Refunds-class is the fastest light fighter in production,
and it achieves this by the elegant engineering strategy of "not installing
anything that slows it down." Hull reinforcement? Heavy. Redundant systems?
Extra mass. Comfortable seats? That's what speed is for — you won't be
sitting long.

voidborn_accretion.name: Accretion
voidborn_accretion.description: Voidborn gas harvester. The collection membrane breathes the nebula.
voidborn_accretion.lore:
The Accretion-class does not scoop gas so much as inhale it. The organic
collection membrane extends from the hull like a vast translucent lung,
filtering volatile compounds through biological processes the Collective
describes as "osmotic affinity." Nebula gas arrives in the hold already
partially separated by molecular weight. Non-Voidborn engineers who have
examined the membrane describe its structure as "disturbingly pulmonary."

solarian_aether.name: Aether
solarian_aether.description: Solarian gas harvester. Atmospheric collection with real-time composition analysis.
solarian_aether.lore:
Named for the classical element once believed to fill the heavens, the
Aether-class features a proprietary atmospheric intake system that the
design team spent fourteen months optimizing. The onboard spectrometer
identifies gas compositions to nine decimal places — six more than any
commercial application requires, but the Confederacy does not believe
in "good enough."

nebula_appraisal.name: Appraisal
nebula_appraisal.description: Nebula salvage vessel. Recovers wreck components and assesses field value in real-time.
nebula_appraisal.lore:
The Appraisal-class was designed after Federation accountants calculated
that 31% of recoverable wreck value was being lost to "inadequate field
assessment." The oversized cargo bay ensures nothing of value gets left
behind, while the onboard valuation system prices each recovered component
in real-time against current market rates. Appraisal pilots receive a
quarterly bonus based on recovery-to-market-value ratio. The Federation
considers this an incentive. Appraisal pilots consider it a lifestyle.

crimson_frostbite.name: Frostbite
crimson_frostbite.description: Crimson ice breaker. Cracks ice with military-grade thermal systems. Armed, naturally.
crimson_frostbite.lore:
The Frostbite-class applies Crimson Fleet engineering philosophy to
ice extraction: overwhelming force. The thermal lance system doesn't
carefully extract ice — it cracks the target body open and scoops out
what's useful. The weapon mount exists because ice fields attract
competitors, and Crimson Fleet doctrine has opinions about sharing.
Hull armor doubles as thermal shielding when the extraction system
runs at full power. Crimson ice miners are the only ones in the
galaxy who consider "explosive decompression" a valid extraction
technique.

solarian_glacius.name: Glacius
solarian_glacius.description: Solarian cryo-extraction vessel. Certified for ice harvesting with full spectral analysis.
solarian_glacius.lore:
The Glacius-class carries a precision cryo-extraction system that the
design committee spent nine months calibrating. The onboard spectrometer
identifies ice compositions to twelve decimal places — the Confederacy
does not believe in approximation when water purity is at stake. Certified
for operation in all known ice field conditions, with a sixty-page
operations manual that pilots are required to have read. Compliance is
verified annually.

crimson_reaver.name: Reaver
crimson_reaver.description: Crimson gas harvester. Collects volatiles from nebulae. Armed, because of course it is.
crimson_reaver.lore:
Gas harvesting requires patience, specialized equipment, and the
willingness to sit inside a volatile cloud of explosive chemicals.
The Crimson Fleet added a weapon mount because sitting still makes
you a target, and Crimson doctrine has opinions about being a target.
The reinforced hull helps with both the occasional weapons fire and
the more frequent collection system malfunctions. Crimson gas
harvesters have the lowest insurance premiums in the fleet — not
because the job is safe, but because Crimson pilots refuse to file
claims.

voidborn_residuum.name: Residuum
voidborn_residuum.description: Voidborn salvager. Filament-tendrils sort wreckage at the molecular level before it enters the hold.
voidborn_residuum.lore:
The Residuum-class extends filament-thin tendrils into debris fields,
sorting wreckage at the molecular level before it enters the cargo hold.
Pilots report the ship seems to anticipate which fragments contain the
most useful materials, orienting toward them before scans complete. The
Collective considers this unremarkable. The hull's surface temperature
drops three degrees during recovery operations. No one has explained why.

voidborn_rime.name: Rime
voidborn_rime.description: Voidborn ice phaser. Extracts ice through dimensional phase-shifting. Always cold inside.
voidborn_rime.lore:
The Rime-class does not mine ice. It convinces ice to relocate. The
Collective's cryo-extraction system uses localized phase-shifting to
separate ice from its parent body at the molecular level, producing
samples of extraordinary purity. Pilots report that the ship's interior
temperature drops measurably during extraction operations — by exactly
the amount the extracted ice's temperature rises. Voidborn engineers
call this "thermal sympathy." Everyone else calls it "deeply unsettling."
The collection chamber maintains specimens at their original temperature
indefinitely, through means the Collective declines to specify.

nebula_windfall.name: Windfall
nebula_windfall.description: Nebula mining specialist. Exceptional cargo capacity for extended operations.
nebula_windfall.lore:
"A Windfall is what happens when preparation meets opportunity." The
Federation's mining vessel outperforms competitors in one metric that
matters most to traders: how much ore you can bring back per trip. The
expanded cargo hold reduces the number of dock-and-sell cycles needed,
which the Federation has calculated saves 15% in time-cost over a
standard mining shift. This statistic is prominently displayed on the
hull, in case potential customers are watching.

solarian_axiom.name: Axiom
solarian_axiom.description: Light Solarian fighter. Balanced, certified, and thoroughly documented.
solarian_axiom.lore:
The Axiom-class light fighter represents the Solarian approach to combat:
measured, methodical, and equipped with three independent targeting verification
systems. Not the fastest or hardest-hitting ship in its class, but pilots report
an unusually low rate of "unexpected outcomes." The balanced power distribution
allows flexible loadouts, which the Confederacy considers a feature rather than
an inability to commit.

crimson_cleaver.name: Cleaver
crimson_cleaver.description: Entry-level Crimson fighter. Three weapon mounts on a frame this size is excessive. That's the point.
crimson_cleaver.lore:
Three weapon mounts on a hull this small violates six Solarian
engineering standards and two Nebula safety certifications. Crimson
Fleet response to the citations was a single unprintable word. The
Cleaver is where Crimson pilots learn the fleet's core lesson: if
you can see it, you can kill it. If you can kill it, mount more guns
and kill it faster. Cargo space was sacrificed for the third weapon
mount. Nobody in the Crimson Fleet complained.

voidborn_paradox.name: Paradox
voidborn_paradox.description: Voidborn light fighter. Shield-dominant defense, unsettling targeting.
voidborn_paradox.lore:
Enemy pilots report that the Paradox-class seems to be in two places at
once during combat maneuvers. Voidborn engineers explain this as "a natural
consequence of the shield harmonics." The targeting system operates on
principles that even Collective technicians describe as "emergent" rather
than "designed." Remarkably effective despite — or perhaps because of —
nobody fully understanding how it works.

outerrim_warranty_void.name: Warranty Void
outerrim_warranty_void.description: Outer Rim explorer. Goes further than intended on less fuel than expected.
outerrim_warranty_void.lore:
Named for its relationship with every manufacturer whose parts went
into its construction, the Warranty Void is built for pilots who believe
the map's edge is a suggestion, not a boundary. The fuel system has been
modified to extract more range from every cell through techniques that
void approximately seventeen different safety certifications. Rim
explorers don't report their modifications. They report their discoveries.

nebula_frost_dividend.name: Frost Dividend
nebula_frost_dividend.description: Nebula ice hauler. Extracts ice and brings it to market in volume.
nebula_frost_dividend.lore:
"Water is the oil of space, and the Frost Dividend makes us the oil
baron." The Federation's ice harvester maximizes throughput with
oversized collection scoops and cargo bays designed for compressed
ice storage. The reinforced containment system prevents sublimation
losses during transit — every gram harvested is a gram delivered.
The Federation's logistics division calculates that ice operations
generate a 340% return on vessel investment over a standard fiscal
year. This figure appears prominently in the investor prospectus.

voidborn_qualia.name: Qualia
voidborn_qualia.description: Voidborn probe tender. Deploys sensor packages that report things they shouldn't know.
voidborn_qualia.lore:
Qualia — the subjective qualities of conscious experience. The Voidborn
chose this name for their probe tender because the probes it deploys do
not merely detect. They experience. Deployed sensor packages report data
that exceeds their hardware specifications, detecting gravitational
anomalies their mass sensors cannot measure and electromagnetic phenomena
outside their spectral range. Whether the probes are better than their
specs suggest or whether the mothership is interpolating data from
"elsewhere" is a question the Collective answers with silence.

crimson_stiletto.name: Stiletto
crimson_stiletto.description: Fast Crimson scout. Reports back what it finds — if it decides the finding is worth reporting.
crimson_stiletto.lore:
The Crimson Fleet doesn't explore for knowledge — it explores for
targets. The Stiletto is fast enough to outrun anything it can't
outfight and sensor-equipped enough to catalog threats before they
catalog it. Scout pilots in the Crimson Fleet occupy an unusual
position: respected for their courage in going ahead alone, but
quietly suspected of insufficient aggression for choosing to observe
rather than engage. The best Stiletto pilots thread this needle by
providing intelligence so good that the follow-up assault force
arrives already winning.

nebula_yield.name: Yield
nebula_yield.description: Nebula gas harvester. Extracts value from what others fly through.
nebula_yield.lore:
"Other pilots see a nebula. We see quarterly earnings." The Yield-class
is optimized for gas cloud extraction with reinforced collection tanks
and oversized cargo bays for compressed gas storage. The Federation
pioneered the technique of harvesting noble gases from clouds that
competitors dismissed as navigational hazards.

nebula_bonanza.name: Bonanza
nebula_bonanza.description: Nebula heavy mining vessel. Maximum cargo for extended mining operations.
nebula_bonanza.lore:
The Bonanza-class is what happens when Federation engineers optimize
exclusively for "ore hauled per credit spent." No weapons — weapons
take space that could carry cargo. Minimal shields — shields add mass
that reduces fuel efficiency. Maximum hold capacity. Federation mining
operations deploy Bonanzas in convoys with Escort-class guards, because
the math clearly shows that specialized mining plus specialized defense
outperforms any armed-miner hybrid. The Federation put this calculation
in the marketing materials.

outerrim_five_finger_discount.name: Five Finger Discount
outerrim_five_finger_discount.description: Outer Rim raider. Optimized for hit-and-run attacks on slower targets.
outerrim_five_finger_discount.lore:
The Five Finger Discount is the Outer Rim's answer to the question
"what if we built a fighter specifically for taking other people's stuff?"
Fast enough to catch freighters, armed enough to convince them to stop,
and just large enough cargo hold to make the whole enterprise worthwhile.
Rim pilots insist they use these for "opportunistic salvage of abandoned
cargo." Nobody believes them.

crimson_flail.name: Flail
crimson_flail.description: Crimson raider. Hits hard, grabs cargo, vanishes before reinforcements arrive.
crimson_flail.lore:
The Flail is the Crimson Fleet's answer to a philosophical question:
why pay for cargo when you can take it? Enough speed to close on
merchants, enough firepower to crack their shields, and enough cargo
space to haul away the interesting bits. The expanded hold was a late
addition — early Flail pilots kept complaining they had to leave
valuable loot behind. The Fleet's logistics division considers the
Flail a "self-funding asset."

voidborn_fugue.name: Fugue
voidborn_fugue.description: Voidborn courier. Arrives before it should. Passengers recall the trip differently.
voidborn_fugue.lore:
Delivery logs for the Fugue-class contain a statistical anomaly that the
Collective refuses to address: arrival times are occasionally recorded
before departure times. Voidborn couriers attribute this to "clerical
variance." The ship's speed is unremarkable on paper, yet packages
arrive ahead of schedule with uncomfortable regularity. The cargo hold
maintains a constant temperature of exactly two degrees below whatever
the pilot sets.

solarian_precept.name: Precept
solarian_precept.description: Solarian patrol craft. Scanner-heavy law enforcement and inspection vessel.
solarian_precept.lore:
A precept is a rule or principle governing personal conduct — and the
Precept-class exists to ensure those rules are followed. The Confederacy's
patrol vessel carries more sensor equipment than weapons, because the
Solarian philosophy of law enforcement prioritizes detection over
deterrence. The onboard scanning suite can identify cargo manifests,
registration anomalies, and outstanding warrants at distances that make
evasion impractical. One weapon mount provides "persuasive capability"
for non-compliant vessels. The official hail begins: "This is a routine
inspection. Please transmit your documentation."

nebula_venture.name: Venture
nebula_venture.description: Nebula explorer. Long range for finding new markets.
nebula_venture.lore:
The Venture-class was designed for Federation scouts who map new trade
routes and identify untapped markets. The generous fuel reserves enable
deep-space surveys, and the decent cargo hold means the scout can bring
samples back for market analysis. "Discovery is just commerce that hasn't
happened yet," reads the operations manual's first page.

voidborn_absence.name: Absence
voidborn_absence.description: Voidborn stealth shuttle. Difficult to detect, difficult to explain.
voidborn_absence.lore:
The Absence doesn't hide — it simply becomes less relevant to nearby
sensors. Voidborn stealth technology doesn't cloak or jam; it operates on
a principle the Collective calls "perceptual displacement," which they
decline to elaborate on. Other empires' best detection equipment occasionally
registers an Absence as a sensor ghost, a cosmic ray artifact, or
"probably nothing." The cabin is always three degrees too cold.

solarian_corollary.name: Corollary
solarian_corollary.description: Solarian interceptor. Fast-response fighter with enhanced targeting.
solarian_corollary.lore:
A corollary follows naturally from a proven theorem, and the Corollary-class
follows naturally from enemy threat vectors. The fastest armed vessel in
the Solarian personal fleet, it trades cargo and armor for speed and
precision targeting. The fire control system calculates optimal intercept
solutions before the pilot finishes reading the threat assessment.

nebula_leverage.name: Leverage
nebula_leverage.description: Nebula armed trader. Carries its cargo and its own insurance policy.
nebula_leverage.lore:
The Leverage-class fills the gap between unarmed hauler and dedicated
escort — a ship that carries enough cargo to turn a profit and enough
firepower to discourage solo pirates from trying anything. The Federation
calls it "vertical integration of the security supply chain." Independent
traders call it "the one that doesn't need a babysitter." The weapon
mount adds 8% to the hull cost but reduces insurance premiums by 22%.
The Federation considers this math self-evident.

crimson_pike.name: Pike
crimson_pike.description: Fast Crimson interceptor. Catches runners before they reach the jump point.
crimson_pike.lore:
In the Crimson Fleet, cowardice isn't running from a fight — it's
letting someone else run from one. The Pike exists to ensure that
doesn't happen. Oversized engines on a light frame give it the speed
to catch anything short of a dedicated racer. The two weapon mounts
are enough to convince most runners to surrender. Those who don't
surrender provide the Pike's pilot with target practice at closing
range. Crimson instructors call the Pike "the leash."

voidborn_apeiron.name: Apeiron
voidborn_apeiron.description: Voidborn disruption craft. Introduces uncertainty into enemy systems at close range.
voidborn_apeiron.lore:
Named for Anaximander's concept of the boundless and indefinite, the
Apeiron introduces a field of low-grade informational entropy into nearby
systems. Enemy sensor readings become imprecise. Targeting solutions drift.
Communications develop a faint echo that makes speakers doubt their own
words. The Collective insists the Apeiron carries no offensive electronic
warfare equipment — the effect is merely a "natural consequence of the
power systems operating at nominal output." This explanation satisfies
no one except other Voidborn.

crimson_dirk.name: Dirk
crimson_dirk.description: Armed Crimson courier. Delivers packages and ultimatums with equal efficiency.
crimson_dirk.lore:
Even the Crimson Fleet needs things delivered quietly. The Dirk
serves as courier, smuggler, and occasionally deniable operative.
The single weapon mount is modest by Crimson standards — practically
pacifist — but the hidden cargo compartments and reinforced cockpit
suggest this ship's real purpose extends beyond mail delivery. Crimson
diplomats prefer the Dirk for "negotiations." The extra utility slots
are for "diplomatic equipment." Nobody asks follow-up questions.

nebula_escort.name: Escort
nebula_escort.description: Nebula convoy guard. Protects high-value shipments on a per-engagement basis.
nebula_escort.lore:
The Escort-class exists because the Federation did the math: hiring
mercenary escorts costs 12% more per annum than fielding purpose-built
convoy guards. Two weapon mounts and enough speed to intercept raiders
make the Escort a cost-effective deterrent. The Federation markets it
as "insurance with teeth."

nebula_futures.name: Futures
nebula_futures.description: Nebula market runner. Speed and computation for time-sensitive financial operations.
nebula_futures.lore:
The Futures-class carries almost no cargo because its cargo is
information. Market data, contract confirmations, price feeds, and
trade cipher authentication packets — all time-sensitive, all valuable,
all requiring the fastest delivery possible. The oversized CPU array
runs predictive market models in transit, meaning a Futures pilot
knows where to go next before they arrive at their current destination.
The Federation's high-frequency trading operations are built on Futures-
class hulls. The tiny cargo bay holds exactly enough for a contract
signing kit and a bottle of champagne.

outerrim_get_off_my_lawn.name: Get Off My Lawn
outerrim_get_off_my_lawn.description: Outer Rim interceptor. Absurdly fast pursuit craft with minimal everything else.
outerrim_get_off_my_lawn.lore:
Speed 6 on a scale-one hull. The Get Off My Lawn is less a ship and more
an engine with a cockpit bolted to it. Rim engineers stripped everything
that wasn't an engine, a weapon mount, or technically required for pilot
survival — and they were generous in their definition of "technically."
Used to chase down anything that enters Rim space uninvited. The name
reflects the typical Rim attitude toward visitors.

outerrim_bedrock.name: Bedrock
outerrim_bedrock.description: Outer Rim heavy mining frame. Two previous collisions. Structurally sound per the last inspection (probably).
outerrim_bedrock.lore:
The Bedrock-class was designed by the Patchwork's engineering collective
after they noticed that most mining ship failures came from the
components, not the hull. Solution: remove the components. The frame
is oversized, the cargo hold is a repurposed fuel bladder, and the
mining laser mount was welded on by someone who clearly knew what
they were doing. The result is a ship that's harder to break than it
is to understand. Some pilots name theirs. The Patchwork considers
this a good sign.

outerrim_baling_wire.name: Baling Wire
outerrim_baling_wire.description: Outer Rim freighter. Held together by determination and creative engineering.
outerrim_baling_wire.lore:
Named for the universal Rim repair material, the Baling Wire-class
freighter is the cheapest way to move large amounts of cargo long
distances. It's faster than it has any right to be thanks to
oversized engines inherited from a decommissioned racing hull.
The name is both a joke and a maintenance manual — experienced pilots
carry actual baling wire for field repairs.

nebula_bulk_terms.name: Bulk Terms
nebula_bulk_terms.description: Nebula commercial hauling hull. Maximizes cargo, minimizes everything else. Standard Federation contract in ship form.
nebula_bulk_terms.lore:
Federation economists calculated the minimum viable bulk hauler
spec: exactly this much hull, exactly this much cargo, no luxury
provisions. The Bulk Terms-class is the result. No automated cargo
management, no passenger accommodations, no redundant navigation
system — the pilot gets 350 units of hold space and a fuel tank
large enough to reach the next exchange. Cargo generates revenue.
Revenue is the whole point. The Federation considers everything
else negotiable, and negotiated it down to nothing.

outerrim_repo_man.name: Repo Man
outerrim_repo_man.description: Outer Rim jury-rigged excavator. Fast, cheap, extracts ore like it's repossessing it.
outerrim_repo_man.lore:
The Repo Man-class approaches asteroids with the same energy a
repossession agent approaches a delinquent borrower's ship: fast,
efficient, and with zero sentimentality about the process. The
oversized engines inherited from a racing hull make it the fastest
mining vessel in its class, which Rim miners consider the most
important safety feature available. Why armor up when you can just
leave faster than whatever's trying to kill you? The mining system
is aggressive — more "directed demolition" than "precision extraction"
— but speed compensates for waste. Get in, grab what you can, get out.

solarian_foundation.name: Foundation
solarian_foundation.description: Solarian long-range survey hull. Sensor-capable without the expensive arrays. Built from materials the Confederacy can reliably supply.
solarian_foundation.lore:
The Foundation-class exists for survey missions that can't wait
for Hypothesis-class production slots. Lower CPU means surveys run
sequentially rather than in parallel; fewer utility slots mean the
pilot selects which sensor systems matter most. Both were acceptable
compromises. Fuel range was not — the Confederacy classified it
non-negotiable in the first design review, where it survived all
twelve subsequent votes unchanged.

crimson_ordinance.name: Ordinance
crimson_ordinance.description: Crimson mass-production combat hull. Three weapons, no frills, reliable in formation. Built from materials the Fleet actually has on hand.
crimson_ordinance.lore:
The Pact builds the Ordinance when supply lines are short and the
mission cannot wait. No targeting computer, no electronic warfare
suite, no redundant shield emitters — just three cannon mounts on a
hull that can be fabricated from materials the Fleet stockpiles
regardless of trade conditions. Not the ship you want for a
prolonged campaign. Exactly the ship you want when you need six of
them by morning and the components yard is two systems away.

outerrim_brain_freeze.name: Brain Freeze
outerrim_brain_freeze.description: Outer Rim scrappy ice rig. Fast-moving ice harvester assembled from questionable sources.
outerrim_brain_freeze.lore:
The Brain Freeze-class got its name from the side effect of operating
the ice extraction system: the cockpit temperature drops to near
freezing when the saws are running at full speed. Rim engineers
solved this by installing a portable heater duct-taped to the
pilot's armrest. The speed advantage over conventional ice harvesters
means it can work ice fields that would be too dangerous for slower
ships — the Rim's universal solution to safety concerns is "be
somewhere else by the time it becomes a problem."

solarian_meridian.name: Meridian
solarian_meridian.description: Solarian freighter. Efficient cargo management with extensive monitoring.
solarian_meridian.lore:
Every gram of cargo in a Meridian-class freighter is tracked by seven
independent inventory systems. The manifest reconciliation process runs
continuously, comparing predicted mass distribution against actual sensor
readings. Smuggling aboard a Meridian is technically possible but requires
defeating more auditing software than most planetary tax authorities employ.

crimson_morningstar.name: Morningstar
crimson_morningstar.description: Armored Crimson fuel tanker. Supplies the fleet or sells to the desperate at markup.
crimson_morningstar.lore:
Wars run on fuel, and the Morningstar keeps Crimson fleets moving.
The heaviest armor of any scale-2 ship in the Crimson lineup
protects the fuel tanks — not out of safety concerns, but because
losing a tanker loses the operation. Two defense slots layer
protection on an already tough hull. The Morningstar's secondary
role as a mobile fuel depot makes it one of the few Crimson ships
that turns a profit without firing a shot: stranded pilots in deep
space pay whatever the Morningstar captain asks. Fleet accountants
call this "field-expedient pricing." Everyone else calls it
price gouging.

voidborn_apophenia.name: Apophenia
voidborn_apophenia.description: Voidborn pattern hauler. Cargo routing determined by probability fields, not market data.
voidborn_apophenia.lore:
Named for the tendency to perceive meaningful patterns in random data —
which is precisely what non-Voidborn cargo inspectors experience when
trying to audit an Apophenia's hold. The organic hull creates a
micro-environment that other empires' trade goods find inexplicably
beneficial. Perishables last longer, crystalline structures become more
ordered, and volatile compounds stabilize. The navigation system suggests
trade routes based on probability fields rather than market data,
and its recommendations are correct more often than chance allows.

solarian_capacity.name: Capacity
solarian_capacity.description: Solarian tanker. Bulk fuel transport with temperature-controlled containment.
solarian_capacity.lore:
The Capacity-class is named for what it has in abundance. Four independent
fuel containment systems maintain volatile liquids at precisely regulated
temperatures — the monitoring software generates hourly status reports
whether anyone reads them or not. The Confederacy's Safety Commission
required fourteen separate pressure relief mechanisms. The design team
added two more, just to be thorough.

outerrim_junkyard_dog.name: Junkyard Dog
outerrim_junkyard_dog.description: Outer Rim heavy fighter. Fast, mean, assembled from questionable sources.
outerrim_junkyard_dog.lore:
The Junkyard Dog is what happens when Rim engineers get access to
military-grade weapon mounts and racing-grade engines simultaneously.
The fastest heavy fighter in production, it compensates for modest
armor with the simple expedient of not being where the enemy shoots.
Each one is unique — the "production line" is more of a "production
suggestion" — but they all share the same essential quality: they bite.

crimson_permafrost.name: Permafrost
crimson_permafrost.description: Crimson combat ice breaker. Cracks ice bodies with thermal lances while fighting off competitors.
crimson_permafrost.lore:
The Permafrost-class is the Crimson Fleet's answer to ice field
territorial disputes: a vessel that cracks ice and cracks skulls with
equal enthusiasm. The dual thermal lance system operates at
temperatures that would melt through a Solarian safety inspector's
clipboard. Two weapon mounts ensure that anyone attempting to contest
a Crimson ice claim will reconsider their life choices. The hull
armor serves triple duty: combat protection, thermal shielding, and
debris deflection when the extraction system shatters ice bodies at
point-blank range.

nebula_prospectus.name: Prospectus
nebula_prospectus.description: Nebula scout. Maps trade routes and identifies commercial opportunities.
nebula_prospectus.lore:
The Prospectus-class is the Federation's advance scout, deployed to
map uncharted systems and assess their commercial potential before the
freighters arrive. Extended fuel reserves, high speed, and a suite of
market-analysis sensors make it the fastest way to turn unexplored
space into a business plan. The Federation considers a Prospectus the
first line item in any expansion budget.

crimson_ransack.name: Ransack
crimson_ransack.description: Armed Crimson freighter. Carries cargo or carries loot — same ship, different manifest.
crimson_ransack.lore:
The Crimson Fleet doesn't build unarmed freighters. The concept
offends them. The Ransack carries two weapon mounts and enough
armor to survive an ambush long enough to fight back or flee —
though Crimson captains overwhelmingly prefer the former. The cargo
bay is generous by Crimson standards, which means it's modest by
Nebula standards. What the Ransack lacks in raw capacity it makes
up for in the ability to fill its hold with goods acquired through
"aggressive negotiations." The Fleet's logistics division classifies
Ransack crews as either "merchants" or "acquisition specialists,"
depending on the quarter's budget.

crimson_siege_drill.name: Siege Drill
crimson_siege_drill.description: Crimson armored strip miner. Mines asteroids like it's assaulting a fortress.
crimson_siege_drill.lore:
The Siege Drill approaches asteroid mining the way the Crimson Fleet
approaches everything: as a military operation. Two weapon mounts —
one for the mining laser, one for discouraging claim-jumpers. Armor
plating thick enough to shrug off debris impacts and return fire
simultaneously. The mining technique is less "precision extraction"
and more "directed demolition." Crimson miners don't find ore seams.
They find asteroids and take them apart. The hull can absorb enough
punishment to keep mining through a firefight, which Fleet doctrine
considers a core capability, not an edge case.

nebula_caravan.name: Caravan
nebula_caravan.description: Nebula bulk freighter. The backbone of Federation commerce.
nebula_caravan.lore:
The Caravan-class is the ship that built the Federation's trade network.
With the largest cargo capacity in its class and fuel reserves for long
hauls, the Caravan turns a profit on routes that would bankrupt smaller
ships. The three utility slots accommodate trade management systems,
route optimizers, and cargo insurance modules. The single weapon mount
is described in marketing materials as "a deterrent" and in engineering
documents as "an afterthought."

outerrim_chop_shop.name: Chop Shop
outerrim_chop_shop.description: Outer Rim field recovery platform. Two guns keep ownership disputes brief.
outerrim_chop_shop.lore:
Officially designated a "field recovery platform," the Chop Shop has a
reputation that precedes it. Two weapon mounts discourage anyone who
might dispute ownership of a wreck, and the cutting array — assembled
from parts sourced across three empires — can reduce a frigate hull to
cargo-sized chunks in under twenty minutes. The name was supposed to be
temporary. The crews voted to keep it. Station authorities pretend not
to notice that some Chop Shops return from "salvage runs" with
suspiciously fresh debris.

solarian_excavation.name: Excavation
solarian_excavation.description: Solarian precision deep-core drill platform. Extracts ore with methodical efficiency.
solarian_excavation.lore:
The Excavation-class approaches asteroid mining as an engineering
discipline rather than manual labor. The precision deep-core drill
system maps ore veins through subsurface sonar before making a single
cut, reducing waste to near zero. The expanded utility slots accommodate
geological survey instruments that generate real-time mineral composition
reports. Confederacy mining regulations require these reports to be filed
within 48 hours of extraction. The Excavation files them in real time,
because compliance should never be an afterthought.

outerrim_long_haul.name: Long Haul
outerrim_long_haul.description: Outer Rim tanker. Carries fuel to where fuel shouldn't be affordable.
outerrim_long_haul.lore:
The frontier runs on fuel, and the Long Haul delivers it. These tankers
keep Rim outposts operational by hauling fuel cells to stations too remote
for regular supply lines. The massive internal tankage doubles as both
cargo and the ship's own reserves — Rim engineers solved the "how do we
carry more fuel without bigger tanks" problem by making the cargo the
fuel system. Pilots joke that the Long Haul is the only ship that gets
better range the more cargo it carries.

solarian_theorem.name: Theorem
solarian_theorem.description: Solarian heavy fighter. Balanced combat platform with superior electronics.
solarian_theorem.lore:
The Theorem-class heavy fighter proves its design thesis through rigorous
field testing. Where other empires would bolt on more guns, Solarian engineers
added more processing power. The result is a combat vessel whose targeting
solutions are demonstrably optimal, even if the committee that approved the
fire control software took eight months to reach consensus.

crimson_warhammer.name: Warhammer
crimson_warhammer.description: Crimson salvage ship. Strips wrecks with efficiency born from extensive practice.
crimson_warhammer.lore:
The Crimson Fleet produces more wrecks than any other empire, so
naturally they also produce the best ships for cleaning them up.
The Warhammer strips defeated vessels with industrial efficiency —
cutting tools, tractor systems, and a cargo hold sized for rapid
acquisition of battlefield salvage. The two weapon mounts serve
double duty: discouraging competitors who arrive to loot the same
field, and providing "close-range material extraction" when cutting
tools would be too slow. Warhammer crews consider themselves the
Fleet's recycling division. What was theirs is ours. What was ours
is still ours.

solarian_absolute_zero.name: Absolute Zero
solarian_absolute_zero.description: Solarian cryo-extraction platform. Temperature-controlled ice processing with zero waste.
solarian_absolute_zero.lore:
Named for the theoretical lower limit of temperature, the Absolute
Zero-class maintains cryo-containment systems so precise that harvested
ice never exceeds its extraction temperature during processing. The
Confederacy's materials science division contributed the containment
design, which underwent three years of peer review before production
approval. The expanded utility slots accommodate crystallographic
analysis equipment that categorizes ice compositions in real time.
Waste products are recycled. Nothing is discarded. The Confederacy
considers this both a technical standard and a moral principle.

solarian_analysis.name: Analysis
solarian_analysis.description: Solarian salvager. Systematic wreck deconstruction with full material cataloguing.
solarian_analysis.lore:
Where other empires see wreckage, the Solarian Confederacy sees a peer-review
opportunity. The Analysis-class methodically deconstructs debris fields with
the same rigor a lab technician applies to specimens. Every recovered component
is scanned, catalogued, and assessed for reuse potential. The onboard materials
database cross-references over forty thousand known alloy compositions.

crimson_billhook.name: Billhook
crimson_billhook.description: Crimson industrial gas harvester. Pulls volatiles from clouds at scale. Armed against claim-jumpers.
crimson_billhook.lore:
Industrial gas harvesting in Crimson space is a territorial
operation. The Billhook's expanded collection system handles
nebula-scale gas extraction while the single weapon mount
discourages anyone with ideas about sharing. Fleet engineers
designed the collection system to handle volatile compounds that
would burn through a Nebula harvester's safety-compliant tanks —
the Crimson approach to volatile containment involves thicker walls
rather than more sensors. The hull armor doubles as blast shielding
for when containment does fail. Crimson gas harvesters are the only
ones in the galaxy with combat damage on their safety inspection forms.

voidborn_entropy.name: Entropy
voidborn_entropy.description: Voidborn salvage researcher. The hull absorbs wreckage like a wound healing in reverse.
voidborn_entropy.lore:
The Entropy-class approaches wreck sites and extends organic tendrils
that sort debris at the molecular level. Materials flow into the cargo
hold already separated by type, purity, and structural integrity. Other
empires' salvage crews use cutting torches and tractor beams; the
Entropy simply opens its hull and lets the wreckage come to it. Salvage
operators report the ship seems to know which components are most
valuable before scanning is complete.

voidborn_hoarfrost.name: Hoarfrost
voidborn_hoarfrost.description: Voidborn phase-ice harvester. Extracts ice from deep void pockets through dimensional techniques.
voidborn_hoarfrost.lore:
Hoarfrost forms when water vapor deposits directly onto a surface
without passing through a liquid phase — a process the Collective
finds philosophically interesting. The Hoarfrost-class harvests ice
through similar phase-transition techniques applied at industrial
scale. The dimensional extraction system reaches through to ice
deposits that exist partially in adjacent phase-space, accessing
reserves that conventional harvesters cannot detect. Pilots report
that the cargo hold occasionally contains ice that was not present
during the extraction operation. The Collective attributes this to
"delayed phase-resolution" and considers it a bonus.

voidborn_resonance.name: Resonance
voidborn_resonance.description: Voidborn harmonic extractor. Vibrates ore out of asteroids through dimensional frequency matching.
voidborn_resonance.lore:
The Resonance-class mining vessel operates on a principle that Voidborn
engineers describe as "sympathetic frequency extraction" and everyone
else describes as "impossible." The ship generates a harmonic field that
causes ore deposits to separate from their surrounding matrix without
physical contact. Asteroids subjected to Resonance-class operations
develop unusual geometric patterns in their remaining structure. The
Collective considers these patterns aesthetically pleasing. Other
miners consider them evidence that something profoundly unnatural
has occurred.

outerrim_spit_and_prayer.name: Spit and Prayer
outerrim_spit_and_prayer.description: Outer Rim gas harvester. Modified racing hull with collection scoops welded on.
outerrim_spit_and_prayer.lore:
Originally a racing hull that "retired" after an incident the pilot
describes only as "the thing with the asteroid." Rim engineers welded
gas collection scoops to the hull, rerouted the intake manifold, and
declared it an industrial vessel. The speed advantage over conventional
harvesters means it can collect from gas clouds that would be too
dangerous for slower ships — the Rim's answer to risk management is
"be fast enough that the risk can't catch you."

crimson_broadaxe.name: Broadaxe
crimson_broadaxe.description: Crimson heavy fighter. The fleet's workhorse — four weapon mounts and the armor to use them.
crimson_broadaxe.lore:
The Broadaxe is the Crimson Fleet's answer to the question every
empire asks differently: "What does a professional warship look
like?" Four weapon mounts. Armor plating thick enough to shrug off
light fire. A power grid that can run all four weapons simultaneously
without prioritization. Where the Solarians would add sensor packages
and the Voidborn would add shield layers, the Crimson Fleet added a
fourth gun. And then armored the ammunition feeds. This is the ship
that wins engagements through sustained, overwhelming firepower
delivered at ranges close enough to guarantee every shot connects.

outerrim_duct_tape_special.name: Duct Tape Special
outerrim_duct_tape_special.description: Outer Rim field medic vessel. Keeps crews alive using improvisation and stubbornness.
outerrim_duct_tape_special.lore:
Frontier medicine is creative medicine. The Duct Tape Special carries
medical supplies, fabrication tools, and enough spare parts to patch
up ships and crews in deep space where the nearest hospital is three
jumps away. Rim medics are famously resourceful — "it's sterile enough"
is a professional assessment, not a complaint. The speed ensures quick
arrival at disaster sites, and the life support systems are the one
thing on the ship that actually meets manufacturer specifications.

nebula_motherlode.name: Motherlode
nebula_motherlode.description: Nebula high-capacity ore hauler. Brings back more ore per trip than anything in its class.
nebula_motherlode.lore:
The Motherlode-class carries more ore per trip than any other mining
vessel in its size class. The Federation's engineering division
calculated the optimal balance between extraction speed, cargo capacity,
and fuel efficiency, then added 20% more cargo space because the
marketing department insisted. Mining crews report that a full
Motherlode hold represents enough raw materials to build a small
station. The Federation considers this a feature, not an exaggeration.

solarian_nebulae.name: Nebulae
solarian_nebulae.description: Solarian gas harvester. Scaled-up atmospheric processing with spectral analysis.
solarian_nebulae.lore:
The plural of "nebula" and the Confederacy's answer to industrial-scale
gas collection. The Nebulae-class features twin atmospheric intake arrays
and a spectral analysis suite that categorizes collected gases in real time.
The expanded cargo hold stores compressed gases at pressures that required
a twelve-page safety addendum to the operating manual. Certified for
operation in all known atmospheric compositions.

voidborn_nebulous.name: Nebulous
voidborn_nebulous.description: Voidborn void gas condenser. Collects gases that sensors insist aren't there.
voidborn_nebulous.lore:
The Nebulous-class collects gases from regions of space that
conventional sensors register as empty vacuum. The Collective's
collection membrane extends into what Voidborn scientists call
"adjacent phase-space" — a concept they explain with diagrams
that give non-Voidborn physicists headaches. The harvested gases
include compounds not found in any standard chemistry database,
which the Collective catalogs under the designation "emergent
materials." Whether these materials existed before the Nebulous
collected them is a question the Collective considers irrelevant.

nebula_reclaim.name: Reclaim
nebula_reclaim.description: Nebula salvager. One captain's wreckage is another's inventory.
nebula_reclaim.lore:
The Federation's accountants identified wreck salvage as an untapped
revenue stream, and the Reclaim-class was their answer. Oversized cargo
bays and industrial-grade tractor beams strip a derelict hull faster
than most ships can scan one. The Federation prefers the term "asset
recovery specialist" to "salvager" — it polls better with investors.

voidborn_recursion.name: Recursion
voidborn_recursion.description: Voidborn heavy interceptor. Shield layers regenerate impossibly fast.
voidborn_recursion.lore:
The Recursion-class is named for what happens to enemy weapons fire — it
encounters the first shield layer, which redirects it to the second, which
feeds it back to the first. Theoretically this shouldn't work. Practically,
enemy pilots report their weapons doing "nothing, repeatedly." The
Collective's shield engineers have published several papers on the design
that contain more philosophical arguments than equations.

nebula_cold_storage.name: Cold Storage
nebula_cold_storage.description: Nebula bulk ice freighter. Harvests and hauls ice at commercial scale.
nebula_cold_storage.lore:
The Cold Storage-class treats ice like what it is: the most valuable
bulk commodity in space. Water, coolant, fuel components — ice is the
raw material for civilization, and the Federation intends to control
the supply chain. The expanded containment system uses triple-walled
insulation that prevents sublimation losses even during extended
deep-space operations. Federation ice merchants have standing contracts
with every major station in the galaxy. The Cold Storage ensures they
can fill those contracts profitably.

solarian_hypothesis.name: Hypothesis
solarian_hypothesis.description: Solarian explorer. Long-range survey vessel with comprehensive sensors.
solarian_hypothesis.lore:
"Every great discovery begins with a hypothesis, and every hypothesis
begins with going somewhere nobody has catalogued yet." The Hypothesis-class
carries more sensors per ton than any other vessel its size. The expanded
fuel reserves enable deep-space surveys that would strand lesser ships.
Standard issue includes a publication-grade spectrographic analysis suite
and pre-formatted templates for peer-reviewed discovery reports.

crimson_partisan.name: Partisan
crimson_partisan.description: Armed Crimson explorer. Explores new systems and can fight its way back out.
crimson_partisan.lore:
The Crimson Fleet explores for strategic advantage, not scientific
curiosity. The Partisan carries enough weapons to fight its way
out of whatever it finds and enough fuel to get home with the
intelligence. Sensor arrays are military-grade — optimized for
threat detection rather than mineral surveys, though they work
for that too. Partisan captains have standing orders to report
defensible positions, choke points, and resource deposits worth
fighting over. The "exploration" report template has a section
for "recommended force composition for seizure."

nebula_portfolio.name: Portfolio
nebula_portfolio.description: Nebula multi-role vessel. Diversified capabilities for diversified income.
nebula_portfolio.lore:
"Never put all your credits in one venture." The Portfolio-class embodies
the Federation's risk-management philosophy with a flexible design that
can mine, haul, or fight as market conditions demand. Two weapon mounts
handle pirates, generous cargo space handles freight, and the speed
allows profitable courier work between trades. Jack of all trades,
master of profit margins.

nebula_revenue.name: Revenue
nebula_revenue.description: Nebula gas harvester. Industrial-scale atmospheric extraction for serious operators.
nebula_revenue.lore:
The Revenue-class scales up the Yield's gas harvesting concept to
serious industrial capacity. Pressurized cargo bays, redundant
collection arrays, and a hull rated for prolonged nebula exposure
make extended harvesting campaigns viable. The Federation's gas
derivatives market was built on Revenue-class hulls, and the
quarterly dividends have been consistent ever since.

nebula_arbitrage.name: Arbitrage
nebula_arbitrage.description: Nebula fast trader. Speed optimized for exploiting price differences.
nebula_arbitrage.lore:
The Federation officially classifies the Arbitrage as a "rapid commercial
response vessel." Everyone else calls it a smuggler's ship. The speed
to outrun questions, the cargo space to make it worthwhile, and the
fuel range to take indirect routes all serve the pilot who understands
that price differences between stations are opportunities, not accidents.
The hidden compartment is listed on the blueprints as an "auxiliary
document storage facility."

outerrim_bad_influence.name: Bad Influence
outerrim_bad_influence.description: Outer Rim electronic warfare frigate. Jams, spoofs, and generally causes problems.
outerrim_bad_influence.lore:
The Bad Influence takes the Rim approach to electronic warfare: if you
can't outgun them, confuse them until they outgun themselves. Built on
a racing chassis with an absurd amount of processing power crammed into
every spare cavity, it's fast enough to stay at jamming range and smart
enough to make enemy targeting systems deeply unreliable. Rim EW doctrine
can be summarized as "make the other guy's day worse."

voidborn_confluence.name: Confluence
voidborn_confluence.description: Voidborn explorer. Drawn to anomalies. The ship suggests destinations.
voidborn_confluence.lore:
Confluence pilots learn quickly that the ship has opinions about where to
go next. The navigation system presents "suggested waypoints" that
correspond to no known database — yet invariably lead to something
interesting. Whether the ship is detecting subtle gravitational signatures
or simply knows things it shouldn't, the Collective declines to clarify.
The expanded fuel reserves were a practical concession: Confluences tend
to wander.

crimson_gauntlet.name: Gauntlet
crimson_gauntlet.description: Crimson prize fighter. Built for duels — flashy, tough, and terrifying in single combat.
crimson_gauntlet.lore:
In the Crimson Fleet, disputes between captains of equal rank are
settled in ship-to-ship combat. The Gauntlet exists for exactly this
purpose: a vessel purpose-built for single combat where both pilots
know the other is trying to kill them. Higher shields than most
Crimson ships (a concession to the spectacle — duels should last
long enough to watch), heavy armor, and three weapon mounts for
aggressive loadouts. Gauntlet duels are broadcast fleet-wide and
wagered upon extensively. The Fleet considers this a morale-building
activity and a legitimate promotion mechanism. Winners keep their
opponent's ship.

solarian_triage.name: Triage
solarian_triage.description: Solarian medical frigate. Emergency response with diagnostic excellence.
solarian_triage.lore:
The Triage-class carries no weapons — the Confederacy considers it a violation
of medical ethics to arm a hospital ship, even in a war zone. What it lacks in
offensive capability, it compensates for with the most comprehensive diagnostic
suite available at its tonnage. The dual life support units maintain separate
environments for patients of different species. The medical AI's bedside
manner has been described as "clinically precise and emotionally theoretical."

nebula_benefit.name: Benefit
nebula_benefit.description: Nebula support frigate. Crew wellness is a productivity multiplier.
nebula_benefit.lore:
The Federation frames the Benefit-class as a humanitarian investment,
but the internal memos are candid: a healthy crew operates at 23%
higher efficiency. Four utility slots accommodate medical bays,
diagnostic systems, and pharmaceutical synthesizers. The double
shields protect a vessel that can't afford to take fire while treating
casualties. Crew insurance premiums drop 30% on fleets that include
a Benefit — which, the Federation points out, makes it pay for itself.

crimson_cudgel.name: Cudgel
crimson_cudgel.description: Crimson drone carrier. Lets someone else take the hits while you direct the swarm.
crimson_cudgel.lore:
Even the Crimson Fleet recognizes the tactical value of not being
in the line of fire — they just prefer to have something else in
the line of fire instead of retreating. The Cudgel deploys combat
drones that swarm targets while the carrier itself stays at a
comfortable distance. Crimson drone doctrine is aggressive: drones
are expendable assets launched in overwhelming numbers. The Cudgel's
unusually high utility slot count accommodates drone bays, drone
repair systems, and drone ammunition fabricators. A fully loaded
Cudgel can sustain a swarm long enough to overwhelm targets several
classes above its own weight.

voidborn_dualism.name: Dualism
voidborn_dualism.description: Voidborn heavy fighter. Two shield arrays operate on contradictory principles simultaneously.
voidborn_dualism.lore:
The Dualism mounts two distinct shield generators operating on mutually
exclusive principles. By conventional physics, they should cancel each
other out. Instead, the overlap zone produces what Voidborn physicists
call "constructive impossibility." Enemy weapons that penetrate one
shield layer find themselves reinforcing the other. Solarian analysts
have published seventeen papers proving this cannot work and forty-three
incident reports confirming that it does.

solarian_syllogism.name: Syllogism
solarian_syllogism.description: Solarian electronic warfare frigate. Disrupts enemy systems through superior processing.
solarian_syllogism.lore:
The Syllogism-class proves that in modern space combat, the most dangerous
weapon is a sufficiently powerful processor running sufficiently clever
software. Solarian electronic warfare doctrine emphasizes "informational
superiority" — knowing exactly what the enemy is doing while ensuring they
know as little as possible about you. The massive CPU array supports
simultaneous jamming, spoofing, and real-time tactical analysis.

outerrim_terms_and_conditions.name: Terms and Conditions
outerrim_terms_and_conditions.description: Outer Rim smuggler. Hidden compartments outnumber visible ones.
outerrim_terms_and_conditions.lore:
Named for what you should have read before buying one, the Terms and
Conditions-class features more hidden compartments than a magician's
stage cabinet. The hull panels conceal cavities, the life support
ducting has removable sections, and the engine housing contains spaces
that officially don't exist. At speed 5 with good fuel range, customs
vessels rarely get close enough to search. On the rare occasion they
do, the scan-resistant hull lining ensures they find exactly as much
as the pilot wants them to find.

solarian_empirica.name: Empirica
solarian_empirica.description: Solarian research corvette. Because data collection should never stop.
solarian_empirica.lore:
The Empirica-class embodies the Confederacy's conviction that knowledge
is a strategic resource. Where other empires field warships at this tonnage,
the Solarians field a mobile laboratory. Five utility slots accommodate
spectrographic analyzers, sample containment systems, and the processing
power to run publication-grade analysis in the field. The onboard research
AI maintains a continuously updated bibliography and will flag potential
discoveries that merit formal investigation. Unarmed, because the
Confederacy maintains that the pursuit of knowledge should not require
the threat of violence — though it does require extensive paperwork.

outerrim_dust_devil.name: Dust Devil
outerrim_dust_devil.description: Outer Rim blockade runner. The fastest ship in its class, bar none.
outerrim_dust_devil.lore:
Speed 6. Nothing else in its size class comes close. The Dust Devil
achieves this through engineering choices that would get Rim shipwrights
arrested in Solarian space: oversized engines, stripped safety systems,
fuel injectors running beyond rated pressure, and a hull so light it
practically qualifies as decorative. Used by the Rim's most daring
smugglers, couriers, and anyone else who believes the solution to
every problem is "go faster." They're not always right. But they're
always gone before you can argue the point.

crimson_falchion.name: Falchion
crimson_falchion.description: Reluctant Crimson EW platform. Jamming is just attacking their sensors instead of their hull.
crimson_falchion.lore:
The Crimson Fleet nearly didn't build an electronic warfare ship.
The concept of attacking something you can't see felt uncomfortably
close to sneaking. The compromise: reframing EW as "attacking their
sensors." The Falchion doesn't hide or deceive — it burns out enemy
electronics with focused interference powerful enough to physically
damage sensor arrays. Crimson EW is less subtle than Voidborn shadow
games but considerably more destructive. A Falchion's jamming suite
doesn't confuse your targeting computer — it sets your targeting
computer on fire. Fleet doctrine permits this as a legitimate form
of direct combat.

voidborn_penumbra.name: Penumbra
voidborn_penumbra.description: Voidborn electronic warfare frigate. Operates in the space between detection and certainty.
voidborn_penumbra.lore:
The Penumbra occupies the twilight between visibility and invisibility,
between signal and noise. Its electronic warfare suite doesn't jam enemy
sensors so much as make them question their own readings. Fleet commanders
report that engagements involving a Penumbra have a dreamlike quality —
targets appear where they shouldn't, distances seem uncertain, and time
itself becomes slightly unreliable. The Collective considers this "working
as intended."

nebula_surplus.name: Surplus
nebula_surplus.description: Nebula mobile trade depot. Sets up a marketplace anywhere there's demand.
nebula_surplus.lore:
The Surplus-class is a marketplace on a hull. Its oversized cargo holds
store inventory for field trading, while the CPU array runs exchange
matching engines, price feeds, and automated market-making algorithms.
Where stations are scarce, a Surplus pilot becomes the economy — setting
prices, filling orders, and taking a cut of every transaction. The
Federation markets the Surplus as "entrepreneurship infrastructure."
Independent traders describe it less charitably as "that guy who shows
up at every mining site with a markup."

voidborn_hysteresis.name: Hysteresis
voidborn_hysteresis.description: Voidborn research corvette. A mobile laboratory that remembers every experiment it has conducted.
voidborn_hysteresis.lore:
Hysteresis: the dependence of a system's output on its history, not
merely its current input. The Hysteresis-class research corvette embodies
this principle — its organic computational substrate retains the context
of every experiment conducted aboard, and subsequent research benefits
from an institutional memory that improves with age. Older Hysteresis
vessels produce measurably better results than new ones, which the
Collective attributes to "accumulated wisdom" and other empires attribute
to "the ship getting smarter, which is concerning." The Collective
considers both descriptions accurate.

voidborn_anamnesis.name: Anamnesis
voidborn_anamnesis.description: Voidborn consciousness vessel. Not a medbay — a mind-bay.
voidborn_anamnesis.lore:
Named for Plato's concept of recollection — knowledge the soul possessed
before birth — the Anamnesis-class does not heal bodies. It maintains
minds. The ship's neural interface suite can stabilize degrading
