consciousness patterns, back up cognitive states, and facilitate transfers
between substrates that the Collective considers routine and other empires
consider existentially terrifying. Patients emerge not just repaired but
clarified, reporting that their thoughts feel "more coherent" afterward.
Whether this means the Anamnesis restores minds or edits them is a
distinction the Collective does not acknowledge.

nebula_franchise.name: Franchise
nebula_franchise.description: Nebula drone carrier. Deploys autonomous units to multiply operational capacity.
nebula_franchise.lore:
The Franchise-class applies the Federation's core business principle
to combat: scale through delegation. A single pilot commands a swarm
of autonomous drones for mining, defense, or area denial. The massive
CPU array coordinates drone operations while the cargo bay stores
replacement units. The Federation's marketing materials describe it
as "a fleet in a single hull" — which is accurate, if you squint.

crimson_halberd.name: Halberd
crimson_halberd.description: Crimson assault ship. Doesn't destroy targets — captures them intact for the Fleet.
crimson_halberd.lore:
The Halberd-class exists because the Crimson Fleet learned that
destroying a ship destroys its cargo. The armored prow, magnetic
clamps, and breaching systems allow a Halberd to lock onto a
target hull and deliver boarding parties while its own weapons
suppress point defense. The extra armor isn't just protection — it's
the battering ram. Halberd pilots train for close-range combat where
weapons overlap with grappling range. In Crimson doctrine, a captured
ship is worth more than a destroyed one. The Halberd makes that
calculation practical.

solarian_postulate.name: Postulate
solarian_postulate.description: Solarian drone carrier. Deploys autonomous units from a well-documented command platform.
solarian_postulate.lore:
The Postulate-class takes a position and then proves it with drones. Solarian
drone doctrine requires each autonomous unit to file a mission report upon
return, and the Postulate's processing core dutifully catalogues them all.
The four utility slots support a variety of drone management and electronic
warfare configurations. Each drone's behavioral parameters are individually
peer-reviewed before deployment authorization.

outerrim_swarm_boss.name: Swarm Boss
outerrim_swarm_boss.description: Outer Rim light drone carrier. Launches salvaged drones at inconvenient velocities.
outerrim_swarm_boss.lore:
Rim drone engineering follows a simple principle: quantity has a quality
all its own. The Swarm Boss carries a complement of drones assembled from
whatever was cheap, available, and approximately the right shape. No two
drones are identical, their firmware is a patchwork of three different
operating systems, and their coordination protocols are best described as
"enthusiastic." What they lack in precision they make up for in numbers
and the sheer bewilderment they cause.

voidborn_parallax.name: Parallax
voidborn_parallax.description: Voidborn phase scout. Briefly exists in two reference frames simultaneously.
voidborn_parallax.lore:
The Parallax is the Collective's first production phase-technology vessel.
Its drive system allows the ship to briefly occupy two positions in
space-time, viewing the same location from two reference frames. The
practical application is reconnaissance that is literally impossible to
intercept — the version of the ship collecting data is not in the same
reference frame as the version that could be detected. Pilots describe
the experience as "looking at yourself from across the room while also
being in the room." The Collective's training manual devotes an entire
chapter to managing the resulting nausea.

nebula_consortium.name: Consortium
nebula_consortium.description: Nebula luxury liner. Passenger comfort rated highest in the sector.
nebula_consortium.lore:
The Consortium-class represents the Federation's belief that commerce
extends beyond cargo holds. Premium passenger transport, diplomatic
functions, and "relationship-building excursions" (the Federation's
term for business trips) all require a vessel that communicates success.
The panoramic observation lounge, climate-controlled cargo bays, and
comprehensive entertainment system serve a practical purpose: happy
clients sign bigger contracts.

voidborn_interstice.name: Interstice
voidborn_interstice.description: Voidborn stealth recon. Hides in the dimensional gaps between things.
voidborn_interstice.lore:
The Interstice exploits what Voidborn physicists call "dimensional
lacunae" — the gaps between the layers of space-time that other empires'
physics doesn't acknowledge exist. The ship slides into these gaps and
observes normal space from a perspective that has no meaningful physical
address. Sensors searching for an Interstice are searching for something
that is, in a very literal sense, not anywhere. The ship's own sensors,
however, maintain perfect clarity from their non-position. Pilots require
extensive psychological screening. The Collective's explanation:
"Some minds find non-existence uncomfortable."

outerrim_gas_trawler.name: Gas Trawler
outerrim_gas_trawler.description: Outer Rim scrappy gas collector. Covers wide gas clouds quickly. Speed is the strategy.
outerrim_gas_trawler.lore:
The Gas Trawler's speed 3 rating makes it the fastest gas harvester in
its tier by a comfortable margin. Rim gas miners exploit this by trawling
through gas clouds at velocity, collection scoops open, grabbing what
they can before slower competition even arrives. The technique is
wasteful — plenty of gas gets missed — but the Trawler covers so much
ground that volume compensates for efficiency. The single weapon mount
is practical rather than aggressive: gas clouds near the frontier attract
claim disputes, and the Trawler's preferred resolution is to shoot once,
collect faster, and leave.

outerrim_ice_trawler.name: Ice Trawler
outerrim_ice_trawler.description: Outer Rim fast ice harvester. Gets to ice fields first, grabs what it can, moves on.
outerrim_ice_trawler.lore:
Same philosophy as every other Rim industrial vessel: get there first,
take what you can, leave before trouble arrives. The Ice Trawler's speed
3 rating lets it reach ice fields ahead of heavier competitors and start
harvesting while they're still in transit. The technique is crude —
thermal cutters slice ice chunks free and tractor beams haul them into
cargo bays without much regard for precision. What it loses in
extraction quality, it gains in speed of operations. The hull is
tougher than the gas variant because ice extraction generates more
debris, and the weapon mount discourages competitors who arrive late
and find the Rim already there.

crimson_gas_raider.name: Gas Raider
crimson_gas_raider.description: Crimson armored gas harvester. Collects volatiles from contested clouds. Armed, inevitably.
crimson_gas_raider.lore:
Gas clouds attract competition. The Gas Raider was designed for the
Crimson Fleet's approach to competition: harvest what you can, shoot
anyone who objects, harvest what they leave behind. Two weapon mounts on
a gas harvester is excessive by every other empire's standards, which the
Crimson Fleet considers a compliment. The armor plating protects the
collection tanks from weapons fire — losing a full cargo of volatiles to
a lucky shot is the only failure mode Crimson gas miners take seriously.
Speed 2 means it can reposition quickly between contested cloud
pockets.

crimson_thermal_lance.name: Thermal Lance
crimson_thermal_lance.description: Crimson heavy ice breaker. Military-grade thermal systems crack ice formations by brute force.
crimson_thermal_lance.lore:
The Thermal Lance-class applies Crimson engineering philosophy to ice
extraction at scale: overwhelming thermal force. The lance system doesn't
carefully extract — it superheats the target ice body until it fractures
along stress lines, then scoops the fragments. Two weapon mounts exist
because ice fields near Crimson space are contested territory, and the
Fleet does not negotiate over resources. The hull armor doubles as
thermal shielding when the lance runs at maximum output. Crimson ice
miners consider "controlled explosive decompression" a standard
extraction technique at this scale.

outerrim_junk_convoy.name: Junk Convoy
outerrim_junk_convoy.description: Outer Rim bulk hauler. Three decommissioned ships welded together. Ugly but enormous.
outerrim_junk_convoy.lore:
The Junk Convoy is exactly what it looks like: multiple decommissioned
freighter hulls welded together with structural bracing and connected
by cargo transfer tunnels. The result is hideous, structurally
questionable, and has 520 cargo capacity. Two weapon mounts exist
because frontier convoys get raided, and the Rim's response to piracy
is "arm everything." At 26,000 credits, it's the cheapest T3 bulk
hauler available, because the Rim doesn't pay premium prices for
ships that used to be three other ships. Engineers from every other
empire agree the Junk Convoy shouldn't work. It works anyway.

outerrim_rock_eater.name: Rock Eater
outerrim_rock_eater.description: Outer Rim overclocked mining cruiser. Fast, loud, eats asteroids for breakfast.
outerrim_rock_eater.lore:
The Rock Eater earned its name honestly. The overclocked mining lasers
run hot enough to make the engineering staff nervous, but they chew
through asteroids faster than ships twice its price. Speed 2 lets it
reposition between asteroid belts without wasting a shift. The hull is
thick enough to handle debris impacts from aggressive extraction, and
the single weapon mount exists because frontier miners occasionally need
to remind pirates that they chose the wrong target. At 27,000 credits,
it's the cheapest T3 miner in the galaxy. Outer Rim philosophy: why
pay more when this works fine?

solarian_atmospheric_sampler.name: Atmospheric Sampler
solarian_atmospheric_sampler.description: Solarian advanced gas collection vessel. Precision atmospheric analysis and extraction.
solarian_atmospheric_sampler.lore:
The Atmospheric Sampler doesn't just collect gas — it catalogs every
molecular component in real time. Five utility slots house spectrometers,
composition analyzers, and fractional separation modules that sort gas
streams by purity grade. The Confederacy's gas harvesting doctrine
emphasizes quality over volume: identify the highest-value compounds,
extract them selectively, leave the rest. The CPU handles atmospheric
modeling that predicts gas cloud density patterns, allowing Sampler
captains to position in optimal collection zones.

solarian_cryogenic_survey.name: Cryogenic Survey
solarian_cryogenic_survey.description: Solarian cryo-research vessel. Temperature-controlled extraction identifies rare crystalline compounds.
solarian_cryogenic_survey.lore:
The Cryogenic Survey-class approaches ice harvesting as a materials
science problem. Five utility slots accommodate thermal regulators,
crystallographic analyzers, and fractional extraction modules that
separate ice into component compounds without contamination. The
Confederacy's cryo-research protocols identify rare crystalline
structures that other empires' ice breakers destroy through brute-force
extraction. Slower than some competitors, but the Cryogenic Survey
extracts more value per ice body than anything else in its class.

crimson_siege_breaker.name: Siege Breaker
crimson_siege_breaker.description: Crimson heavy mining cruiser. Drills through asteroids like a battering ram through a gate.
crimson_siege_breaker.lore:
The Siege Breaker approaches asteroid mining the way the Crimson Fleet
approaches everything: as an act of war. Five hundred points of hull
backed by thirty points of armor means this mining cruiser shrugs off
debris fields that would shred lesser ships. Two weapon mounts — one
for the mining laser, one for whatever you point at someone who tries to
steal your claim. The cargo bay is designed to hold raw ore in bulk, not
to organize it neatly. Crimson miners call the Siege Breaker "the
Bludgeon" because it doesn't extract ore — it beats asteroids into
submission.

voidborn_absolute_entropy.name: Absolute Entropy
voidborn_absolute_entropy.description: Voidborn ice phaser. Phase-shifts through ice to extract materials from deep cores without breaking the surface.
voidborn_absolute_entropy.lore:
The Absolute Entropy-class harvests ice through dimensional phase-
shifting — the extraction system reaches through the surface of an
ice body without disturbing it, pulling target materials directly from
the interior. The technique leaves ice formations structurally intact,
which the Collective considers both efficient and respectful. Other
empires' ice miners crack, shatter, and melt their targets. The
Voidborn reach inside and take what they need. The ship's name refers
to the theoretical heat death state that dimensional phase-extraction
locally approximates — for a fraction of a second, the extraction zone
experiences conditions that should not exist outside mathematical
models.

solarian_deep_survey.name: Deep Survey
solarian_deep_survey.description: Solarian industrial research miner. Precision deep-core extraction with full geological analysis.
solarian_deep_survey.lore:
The Deep Survey-class represents the Confederacy's conviction that mining
is applied geology. Six utility slots accommodate a full sensor suite,
spectral analyzers, and ore processing modules that identify the optimal
extraction points before the first laser fires. The result is higher
yield per asteroid than any brute-force approach — Solarian miners don't
mine more, they mine smarter. The CPU capacity allows real-time
geological modeling, and the 350-unit cargo bay holds the results of
thorough, methodical extraction operations.

nebula_enterprise.name: Enterprise
nebula_enterprise.description: Nebula bulk transport. Moves more cargo per credit than anything flying.
nebula_enterprise.lore:
Named for the spirit of commercial ambition rather than any specific
vessel, the Enterprise-class is the Federation's medium-hull cargo
champion. 350 units of cargo space makes multi-station trade runs
profitable on routes that would starve smaller ships. The four utility
slots accommodate trade optimization systems, route planners, and
the Federation's proprietary price prediction algorithms (subscription
required, competitive rates).

voidborn_fold_hauler.name: Fold Hauler
voidborn_fold_hauler.description: Voidborn phase-shifted cargo vessel. Bigger on the inside. Dimensional compression applied to logistics.
voidborn_fold_hauler.lore:
The Fold Hauler-class applies the Collective's dimensional compression
technology to commercial logistics. The cargo bay partially exists in a
pocket dimension, giving the ship significantly more internal volume
than its external dimensions suggest. Other empires' engineers insist
this is impossible. Voidborn quartermasters respond by loading 480 units
of cargo into a hull that external measurements suggest should hold 200.
The dimensional fold requires constant shield power to maintain, which
explains the high shield capacity — if the fold collapses, the cargo
doesn't disappear, but the cleanup is described in Collective
maintenance manuals as "geometrically complicated."

voidborn_phase_collector.name: Phase Collector
voidborn_phase_collector.description: Voidborn gas collector. Harvests anomalous gas signatures that sensors insist don't exist.
voidborn_phase_collector.lore:
The Phase Collector-class harvests gases that other empires cannot
detect, let alone collect. The collection membranes extend partially
into adjacent dimensional layers, capturing exotic compounds that
sublimate from the boundary between dimensions. Ship sensors in the
collection bay frequently report "impossible" gas compositions — noble
gases that shouldn't react, molecular structures with too many bonds,
compounds that exist only at dimensional interfaces. The Collective's
response to inquiries about the nature of these substances is a
standardized non-answer that the questioner somehow finds satisfying.

solarian_compendium.name: Compendium
solarian_compendium.description: Solarian bulk hauler. Maximum cargo throughput with inventory optimization.
solarian_compendium.lore:
The Compendium-class hauls cargo the way the Confederacy does everything:
thoroughly, methodically, and with comprehensive documentation. The
automated cargo management system optimizes load distribution in real time,
adjusting center-of-mass calculations after every container shift. It is
slow. The Confederacy prefers the term "deliberate." Six cargo containers
provide enough space that the inventory manifest alone requires its own
dedicated processor.

voidborn_deep_resonance.name: Deep Resonance
voidborn_deep_resonance.description: Voidborn deep void excavator. Vibrates ore out of asteroids through dimensional frequency matching.
voidborn_deep_resonance.lore:
The Deep Resonance-class extracts ore through a process the Collective
calls "harmonic persuasion" — dimensional frequency generators vibrate
target asteroids at their resonant frequency until the ore separates
from the surrounding rock without mechanical force. Other empires'
mining engineers insist this is physically impossible. Voidborn miners
shrug and fill their cargo bays. The shield capacity protects against
asteroid debris and dimensional resonance feedback. Six utility slots
accommodate the sensor arrays needed to calculate each asteroid's unique
harmonic signature.

nebula_gas_tanker.name: Gas Tanker
nebula_gas_tanker.description: Nebula heavy gas collection vessel. Industrial-scale atmospheric extraction and compression.
nebula_gas_tanker.lore:
The Gas Tanker-class was designed by Federation accountants before it
was designed by engineers. Specification one: maximum gas collection
volume. Specification two: minimum cost per unit collected. Everything
else was negotiable. The result is a slow, unarmed vessel with
enormous pressurized tanks that compress collected gas to densities that
make other empires' safety inspectors nervous. The Federation considers
this an acceptable risk-reward ratio. The Gas Tanker doesn't fight, it
doesn't hurry, and it doesn't come back until the tanks are full.

outerrim_hells_bells.name: Hell's Bells
outerrim_hells_bells.description: Outer Rim cruiser. Fast for a cruiser. Very fast. Suspiciously fast.
outerrim_hells_bells.lore:
Speed 4 on a medium hull. Other empires' naval architects insist this
shouldn't be possible without cutting something critical. They're
correct — the Hell's Bells sacrifices armor, shields, and what
Solarian engineers call "structural margin" in exchange for engines
that can outrun most interceptors. Rim pilots consider the ability
to choose when and where to fight worth more than the ability to
absorb punishment. The name comes from what you hear when the
engines go to full power.

nebula_ice_dividend.name: Ice Dividend
nebula_ice_dividend.description: Nebula bulk ice freighter. Carves ice chunks and hauls them to market at commercial scale.
nebula_ice_dividend.lore:
The Ice Dividend-class applies the Federation's bulk commodity
philosophy to frozen resources. Enormous insulated cargo bays maintain
harvested ice at extraction temperature during transit — no melting, no
waste, no lost profit margin. Unarmed because the Federation considers
weapons on ice haulers a waste of mass that could carry more product.
The cryo-containment systems are the most sophisticated in the galaxy,
not because the Federation cares about science, but because thawed ice
is worth less than frozen ice.

crimson_trebuchet.name: Trebuchet
crimson_trebuchet.description: Crimson bulk hauler. Carries the spoils of victory. Armed, because even haulers fight here.
crimson_trebuchet.lore:
Someone has to move the ore, the weapons, and the spoils of war.
In the Crimson Fleet, that someone still carries guns. The Trebuchet
is the largest cargo vessel the Fleet builds — 350 capacity with
the armor to haul it through hostile space. Two weapon mounts deter
the opportunistic. The heavy hull armor deters the determined. Nebula
Trade Federation analysts have calculated that a Trebuchet convoy
is actually cost-competitive with their own haulers when you factor
in the security escort the Nebula ships require and the Trebuchet
doesn't.

nebula_bonanza_king.name: Bonanza King
nebula_bonanza_king.description: Nebula mega ore hauler. Extracts and stores more ore per trip than anything in its class.
nebula_bonanza_king.lore:
The Bonanza King-class is the Federation's answer to a simple question:
why make two trips when you could make one? At 450 cargo capacity, it
hauls more ore per trip than most T3 ships can carry total. Zero weapon
mounts freed the tonnage for additional cargo systems and processing
infrastructure. Federation doctrine considers armed mining ships a
misallocation of resources — escorts exist for a reason, and ore in the
hold is worth more than guns on the hull. The Bonanza King's economics
are simple: more cargo, more revenue, more often.

outerrim_hold_my_beer.name: Hold My Beer
outerrim_hold_my_beer.description: Outer Rim salvage cruiser. For recovering things from places you shouldn't be.
outerrim_hold_my_beer.lore:
Named for the traditional Rim phrase that precedes every great
engineering decision, the Hold My Beer-class is built for deep-space
salvage operations in places other empires consider too dangerous,
too remote, or too close to things that explode. The speed ensures
quick exits when the situation deteriorates (a when, not an if, in
Rim operating philosophy). The cargo capacity accommodates the kind
of hauls that make the risk worthwhile.

solarian_promenade.name: Promenade
solarian_promenade.description: Solarian liner. Diplomatic transport with academic conference facilities.
solarian_promenade.lore:
The Promenade-class is the Confederacy's idea of luxury travel: comfortable
cabins, excellent life support, and a fully equipped lecture hall. Passenger
amenities include a research-grade library, three seminar rooms, and a
lounge with panoramic viewports certified to withstand "all anticipated
environmental conditions." Popular for inter-system academic conferences,
faculty relocations, and the occasional diplomatic function where everyone
pretends the after-dinner talk is optional.

solarian_quorum.name: Quorum
solarian_quorum.description: Solarian cruiser. Balanced multi-role warship with committee-approved firepower.
solarian_quorum.lore:
The Quorum-class cruiser was designed by consensus, which in the
Confederacy means it took four years and three redesigns. The result
is a ship that does everything well and nothing brilliantly — exactly
as the seventeen-member design committee intended. The balanced loadout
supports fleet roles from patrol to escort to independent operations.
Every system has a backup, every backup has documentation, and every
document has been reviewed by at least two committees.

nebula_reserve.name: Reserve
nebula_reserve.description: Nebula fuel tanker. Keeps the fleet moving and the credits flowing.
nebula_reserve.lore:
The Reserve-class keeps Federation convoys operational on routes that
would strand lighter ships. Massive internal fuel bunkerage and
pressurized cargo holds for fuel resale make the Reserve both a
logistical asset and a profit center. The Federation's fuel pricing
at deep-space waypoints is described internally as "competitive"
and externally as "the only option for 50 lightyears."

crimson_warmaul.name: Warmaul
crimson_warmaul.description: Crimson main battle cruiser. Five weapon mounts. The backbone of any serious Crimson operation.
crimson_warmaul.lore:
The Warmaul is where the Crimson Fleet stops pretending and starts
deploying. Five weapon mounts deliver sustained broadside firepower
that exceeds anything else at this scale. The armor is thick enough
to wade through fighter screens. The power grid runs all five weapons
without compromise. Where other empires' cruisers are multi-role
platforms with balanced stats, the Warmaul is a gun platform that
happens to have engines. Crimson fleet commanders consider the
Warmaul the minimum acceptable response to any tactical situation.
Everything lighter is "reconnaissance in force."

voidborn_dissolution.name: Dissolution
voidborn_dissolution.description: Voidborn salvager. Its organic hull extends across a debris field like a membrane, leaving nothing behind.
voidborn_dissolution.lore:
The Dissolution-class does not salvage wrecks so much as unmake them.
Its organic hull extends across a debris field like a membrane, and
materials pass through in order of molecular complexity — metals first,
then composites, then exotic alloys. The process leaves nothing behind.
Not even dust. Operators of other empires' salvage ships have reported
arriving at wreck sites after a Dissolution to find the debris field
geometrically perfect — as though the wreckage that remained had been
deliberately arranged. The Collective does not comment on this
observation.

crimson_bonesaw.name: Bonesaw
crimson_bonesaw.description: Crimson heavy salvager. Industrial cutting arrays backed by warship armor and three weapon mounts.
crimson_bonesaw.lore:
The Bonesaw-class is what happens when the Crimson Fleet applies warship
design philosophy to industrial vessels. Three weapon mounts protect a
cutting array that can disassemble a cruiser hull in under an hour. The
cargo bay is reinforced to mil-spec — not for structural reasons, but
because Bonesaw crews have a documented tendency to ram debris that proves
difficult to cut. Fleet Command classifies the Bonesaw as a support
vessel. Bonesaw crews classify themselves as apex predators of the wreck
field.

outerrim_midlife_crisis.name: Midlife Crisis
outerrim_midlife_crisis.description: Outer Rim liner. Frontier luxury for people whose definition of luxury is flexible.
outerrim_midlife_crisis.lore:
The Midlife Crisis is what passes for a luxury liner on the Rim. The
viewing lounges have actual windows (reinforced glass, not portholes).
The quarters have doors that close (most of the time). The bar serves
drinks that are technically beverages (by frontier health standards).
It's fast enough to outrun boredom and has enough fuel to reach the
interesting parts of the galaxy. Passengers are mostly retired
prospectors spending their earnings, adventurers who can't sit still,
and the occasional fugitive enjoying the view.

solarian_remedy.name: Remedy
solarian_remedy.description: Solarian repair ship. Mobile maintenance platform with diagnostic precision.
solarian_remedy.lore:
The Remedy-class diagnoses ship malfunctions the way a Solarian physician
diagnoses patients: exhaustively, with multiple independent verification
methods, and with a written report afterward. Five utility slots accommodate
repair drones, welding arrays, and material fabrication systems. The
diagnostic CPU identifies structural failures down to the molecular level.
Field-repaired vessels are issued a "Certificate of Provisional Airworthiness"
that somehow manages to be both reassuring and ominous.

voidborn_singularity.name: Singularity
voidborn_singularity.description: Voidborn cruiser. Shield layers that shouldn't be possible. Crew reports mild temporal displacement.
voidborn_singularity.lore:
The Singularity-class carries shield arrays that produce readings other
empires' sensors classify as "errors." Four defense slots support a
layered shield architecture that the Collective describes as "recursive
probability reduction." Enemy weapons officers report that targeting
solutions decay faster than they can be computed. Crew members occasionally
experience mild deja vu, which the ship's manual lists under "features"
rather than "known issues."

crimson_stockade.name: Stockade
crimson_stockade.description: Armored Crimson transport. Supplies run through warzones — armor is a logistics requirement.
crimson_stockade.lore:
Supply lines in Crimson space run through contested territory,
because all territory in Crimson space is contested. The Stockade
carries ammunition, fuel, repair materials, and replacement weapons
to forward positions through environments where unarmed ships
don't survive. Thirty points of armor and two weapon mounts make
the Stockade harder to crack than most empires' dedicated warships
at this scale. Crimson logistics officers consider safe supply routes
a myth and design accordingly. An entire Crimson military doctrine —
"the supply line shoots back" — was built around the Stockade's
capabilities.

nebula_tariff.name: Tariff
nebula_tariff.description: Nebula energy cruiser. The cost of crossing Federation space without a trade license.
nebula_tariff.lore:
The Tariff-class is what happens when the Federation decides to protect
its trade lanes with something heavier than a light escort. Three weapon
mounts, strong shields, and surprising speed for its tonnage make the
Tariff a credible threat to pirates and a reassuring sight for convoy
captains. The energy barrier systems drain power from approaching
hostiles before conventional weapons even fire. The name is deliberate —
crossing Federation space without permission costs you one way or another.

solarian_taxonomy.name: Taxonomy
solarian_taxonomy.description: Solarian heavy salvager. Industrial-scale debris field processing with assembly-line material sorting.
solarian_taxonomy.lore:
The Taxonomy-class is the Confederacy's dedicated heavy salvage platform.
Where the Analysis handles individual wrecks, the Taxonomy processes entire
debris fields — cataloguing, sorting, and storing materials with
assembly-line precision. The onboard laboratory can identify alloy
compositions to fourteen decimal places, which no customer has ever
requested but which Solarian engineers consider a basic professional
courtesy. Recovery reports from a Taxonomy operation have been accepted
as evidence in three separate interstellar court proceedings.

outerrim_peer_review.name: Peer Review
outerrim_peer_review.description: Outer Rim research vessel. Science conducted at unsafe velocities.
outerrim_peer_review.lore:
Rim science operates on the principle that if your research vessel is
fast enough, you can study dangerous phenomena up close and still get
away before the phenomena object. The Peer Review carries serious
computational power and sensor arrays that Solarian researchers would
grudgingly respect. The key difference is methodology: Solarian science
is careful, methodical, peer-reviewed. Rim science is "fly close to the
weird thing, take readings, run away, publish if survived." Their
papers are surprisingly well-cited.

solarian_perihelion.name: Perihelion
solarian_perihelion.description: Solarian research vessel. Mobile laboratory with comprehensive analysis suites.
solarian_perihelion.lore:
The Perihelion-class carries more analytical instruments per cubic meter
than any other vessel in production. Six utility slots accommodate
specialized research equipment for fields ranging from astrophysics to
xenobiology. The extensive fuel reserves enable long-duration research
expeditions, and the powerful CPU array processes data sets that would
take a surface laboratory weeks. Published more peer-reviewed papers per
ship-year than any other vessel class in service.

crimson_polearm.name: Polearm
crimson_polearm.description: Long-range Crimson raider. Goes deep behind enemy lines, comes back loaded with intelligence and loot.
crimson_polearm.lore:
The Polearm is the Crimson Fleet's deep-penetration raider — built
for long-range operations behind enemy lines where resupply is
impossible and retreat is measured in days. Extended fuel tanks feed
a range that rivals dedicated exploration vessels, while three
weapon mounts and the Fleet's characteristic heavy armor ensure the
Polearm can fight its way through whatever it encounters. The
generous utility slots accommodate sensor packages, cloaking devices,
and whatever else a captain needs for extended independent operations.
Polearm missions are considered the most prestigious assignments in
the Crimson Fleet — dangerous, self-reliant, and frequently profitable.

nebula_receivership.name: Receivership
nebula_receivership.description: Nebula heavy salvage platform. Recovers entire battlefields' worth of assets in a single operation.
nebula_receivership.lore:
The Receivership-class is the Federation's answer to the question "what
if we could recover an entire battlefield's worth of assets in a single
operation?" The answer, naturally, involved the largest cargo bay the
Federation could fit on a tier-three hull and a proprietary asset-tracking
system that assigns serial numbers to individual hull fragments. Insurance
companies across the sector have designated the Receivership as a preferred
recovery platform, which comes with a 12% premium discount that the
Federation's marketing division mentions at every opportunity.

nebula_syndicate.name: Syndicate
nebula_syndicate.description: Nebula armed freighter. Profitable enough to justify escorts, armed enough to be one.
nebula_syndicate.lore:
The Syndicate-class represents the Federation's pragmatic answer to
piracy: if the cargo is valuable enough to steal, the ship carrying it
should be dangerous enough to discourage the attempt. Three weapon mounts
and respectable shields mean a Syndicate can handle most solo raiders,
while 250 cargo capacity ensures the escort contract pays for itself.
The Federation's actuaries have calculated that a Syndicate reduces
insurance premiums by 40%.

crimson_anvil.name: Anvil
crimson_anvil.description: Crimson field repair ship. Patches hulls under fire because the battle doesn't stop for maintenance.
crimson_anvil.lore:
The Crimson Fleet doesn't retreat for repairs. The Anvil brings
repairs to the fleet — welding hull breaches, replacing armor
plates, and rerouting damaged systems while weapons fire passes
overhead. The cargo hold carries replacement parts and raw materials.
The three defense slots keep the Anvil functional in the combat zone
where it operates. Anvil crews are the only non-combat personnel in
the Crimson Fleet who earn combat honors — because they work in the
same fire as everyone else, just with welding torches instead of
weapons. The single weapon mount is "for morale," according to the
ship's specification document.

outerrim_insurance_fraud.name: Insurance Fraud
outerrim_insurance_fraud.description: Outer Rim assault ship. Hits hard, moves fast, leaves before consequences arrive.
outerrim_insurance_fraud.lore:
The Insurance Fraud-class carries five weapon mounts on a hull that
moves at speed 4 — a combination other empires' naval architects call
"structurally irresponsible." Rim assault doctrine boils down to
"arrive unexpectedly, shoot everything, leave before they reorganize."
The name reflects what happens to enemy fleet insurance premiums when
one of these shows up in-system. Hull integrity is secondary to the
philosophy that a fight resolved in three seconds needs no armor.

voidborn_noumenon.name: Noumenon
voidborn_noumenon.description: Voidborn deep-space explorer. Detects phenomena that shouldn't exist.
voidborn_noumenon.lore:
Named for Kant's concept of the "thing-in-itself" — that which exists
beyond perception — the Noumenon-class carries sensor arrays designed
to detect exactly that. Voidborn deep-space expeditions routinely
report discoveries in systems other empires have already surveyed and
dismissed as empty. Whether the Noumenon perceives things others cannot
or creates phenomena through the act of observation remains, fittingly,
unresolved.

solarian_perigee.name: Perigee
solarian_perigee.description: Solarian expedition vessel. Extended-range deep space exploration platform.
solarian_perigee.lore:
The perigee is the point in an orbit closest to the body being orbited —
the moment of nearest approach. The Perigee-class is built for those
moments: reaching distant phenomena and staying close long enough to study
them properly. Massive fuel reserves, six utility slots for scientific
instruments, and a CPU array capable of processing months of accumulated
survey data. Expedition crews report that the ship's library database
contains more reading material than any crew could finish in a standard
deployment. The Confederacy considers this aspirational.

nebula_survey.name: Survey
nebula_survey.description: Nebula expedition vessel. Deep-space exploration with all the comforts of a corner office.
nebula_survey.lore:
The Survey-class is the Federation's answer to the question: "What's
out there that we haven't monetized yet?" Extended fuel reserves for
months-long expeditions, a high-capacity CPU array for mapping and
analysis, and five utility slots for every conceivable survey instrument.
The comfortable crew quarters are a practical decision — well-rested
explorers discover more profitable resources.

crimson_bastille.name: Bastille
crimson_bastille.description: Crimson interdictor. Nobody leaves the combat zone until the Crimson Fleet says so.
crimson_bastille.lore:
The Bastille solves a problem the Crimson Fleet finds personally
offensive: enemies running away. The warp disruption field prevents
jump drives from engaging, trapping everything within range in
normal space. The Bastille's own defenses are unusually robust for
a Crimson ship — three defense slots and solid shields — because
the ship that prevents escape becomes priority target number one.
Crimson Fleet commanders deploy the Bastille first and build their
battle plan around it. The doctrine is simple: close the door, then
light the room on fire. Nobody leaves until the fleet is done.

solarian_dialectic.name: Dialectic
solarian_dialectic.description: Solarian assault cruiser. Concentrated forward firepower for decisive engagements.
solarian_dialectic.lore:
The Dialectic-class resolves disagreements through superior argumentation —
specifically, five weapon mounts delivering synchronized volleys computed by
a dedicated fire-sequencing processor. The Confederacy's Tactical Doctrine
Board spent two years debating whether concentrated or distributed firepower
was philosophically superior. The Dialectic represents their conclusion: why
not both, with extensive documentation supporting either configuration.

voidborn_gestalt.name: Gestalt
voidborn_gestalt.description: Voidborn drone carrier. The drones may share consciousness with the ship.
voidborn_gestalt.lore:
Gestalt-class drones do not receive commands from the mothership. They
appear to know what the mothership intends before it communicates.
Voidborn engineers explain this as "emergent coordination through shared
substrate" — a phrase that has generated more academic papers than
answers. The drones' behavior in combat is described by other empires
as "unsettlingly organic," moving as schools of fish or flocks of birds
rather than as independent units receiving instructions.

outerrim_money_pit.name: Money Pit
outerrim_money_pit.description: Outer Rim mobile refinery. Processes raw ore in the field. Eats fuel accordingly.
outerrim_money_pit.lore:
The Money Pit earns its name twice: once for the cost to build it,
and again for the fuel it consumes running the onboard refinery. Rim
miners discovered that processing ore in the field eliminates the
round trip to a station, which more than compensates for the Money
Pit's voracious appetite. The refinery equipment was reverse-engineered
from Nebula Collective designs with what Rim engineers describe as
"creative liberties." It runs hot, loud, and slightly off-spec. The
refined materials come out fine. Mostly.

nebula_warranty.name: Warranty
nebula_warranty.description: Nebula repair vessel. Keeping Federation assets in service is cheaper than replacing them.
nebula_warranty.lore:
The Warranty-class is the Federation's mobile maintenance platform,
because every hour a damaged freighter sits idle is revenue lost.
Five utility slots accommodate repair drones, hull printers, and
diagnostic arrays. The generous cargo bay stores replacement parts
at markup prices. The Federation offers "Warranty Extended Service
Contracts" that guarantee priority repair — terms and conditions apply.

voidborn_catharsis.name: Catharsis
voidborn_catharsis.description: Voidborn signal ship. Transmits and receives on frequencies that don't officially exist.
voidborn_catharsis.lore:
The Catharsis-class was designed to investigate a phenomenon the
Collective refers to only as "the Signal" — an anomalous transmission
detected across the Voidborn communications network whose origin,
content, and purpose remain classified. The ship's antenna arrays
operate on frequencies that do not appear in any empire's electromagnetic
spectrum charts. What the Catharsis transmits, and to whom, is unknown.
What it receives is processed through computational substrates that
are physically isolated from all other ship systems, suggesting that
even the Collective exercises caution around whatever it is listening to.
The ship's name — meaning purification through emotional release — is
either deeply meaningful or deliberately misleading. With the Voidborn,
both are equally likely.

voidborn_annihilation.name: Annihilation
voidborn_annihilation.description: Voidborn void assault ship. Does not destroy — it unmakes.
voidborn_annihilation.lore:
The Annihilation-class does not destroy ships in the conventional sense.
Its weapon systems operate on a principle the Collective calls "reciprocal
negation" — targeted dimensional collapse that removes matter from one
reference frame without transferring it to another. Where the matter
goes is a question Voidborn weapons engineers answer with "elsewhere."
The shield array metabolizes incoming energy and channels it to the
weapons, meaning the harder you hit an Annihilation, the more of you
it can unmake. Crimson Fleet tacticians have learned that the only
winning strategy is overwhelming force applied simultaneously from
every direction. The Collective finds this an interesting admission.

crimson_mace.name: Mace
crimson_mace.description: Crimson assault cruiser. Takes capital ships by force. The Fleet's heavy boarding platform.
crimson_mace.lore:
The Halberd takes frigates. The Mace takes everything else. Thirty-
eight points of armor on a hull designed to absorb point-blank
defensive fire while magnetic clamps lock onto the target and
breaching systems cut through capital-grade plating. The Mace
carries enough marines and equipment to seize ships three times its
size — the boarding bays are pressurized, the armory is extensive,
and the medical bay exists because boarding actions produce
casualties. Four weapon mounts suppress point defense during the
approach. Mace captains are the Crimson Fleet's most decorated
officers, because every successful boarding is a capital ship
captured intact.

nebula_monopoly.name: Monopoly
nebula_monopoly.description: Nebula trade cruiser. Protects commercial interests with energy drain weapons and economic leverage.
nebula_monopoly.lore:
The Monopoly-class is the Federation's most feared non-capital warship,
not because of its weapons — though those are adequate — but because
of its electronic warfare suite. The Monopoly's energy drain systems
siphon power from hostile ships, degrading their weapons, shields, and
propulsion simultaneously. Engaging a Monopoly is an exercise in
diminishing returns: the longer the fight continues, the weaker you
get and the stronger it gets. The Federation calls this "hostile
acquisition." Pirates call it "the reason we avoid Federation convoys."
The four utility slots run market analysis even during combat, because
the Federation never stops calculating.

outerrim_probable_cause.name: Probable Cause
outerrim_probable_cause.description: Outer Rim blockade runner. When subtlety fails, speed succeeds.
outerrim_probable_cause.lore:
Speed 5 on a medium hull. The Probable Cause represents the Outer Rim's
finest tradition of solving every problem by going faster. Named by a
pilot who pointed out that "existing near a Probable Cause is itself
probable cause for a customs inspection," these ships run blockades,
deliver contraband, carry diplomatic pouches, and generally make border
authorities very unhappy. The fuel reserves support indirect routes
through systems nobody patrols because nobody expected anyone to go there.

voidborn_solipsism.name: Solipsism
voidborn_solipsism.description: Voidborn intelligence platform. Is the data real, or did the ship imagine it?
voidborn_solipsism.lore:
Solipsism: the philosophical position that only one's own mind is sure
to exist. The Solipsism-class intelligence platform collects signals,
communications, and data patterns from across entire star systems,
then processes them through a computational substrate that other empires
cannot audit or verify. Intelligence reports from a Solipsism are
consistently accurate yet contain insights that could not have been
derived from the intercepted data alone. Whether the ship is inferring
brilliantly or fabricating convincingly — and whether there is a
meaningful difference — is a question the Collective finds amusing
rather than troubling.

solarian_concordance.name: Concordance
solarian_concordance.description: Solarian command ship. Coordinates fleet operations through superior processing.
solarian_concordance.lore:
The Concordance-class is the Confederacy's answer to the question of
how to coordinate a fleet of ships whose captains all believe they
know best. The answer, naturally, is more processing power. The
massive CPU array runs tactical models, logistics simulations, and
diplomatic communication channels simultaneously. Fleet commanders
report that the Concordance's tactical recommendations are "usually
correct and always exhaustively documented."

crimson_juggernaut.name: Juggernaut
crimson_juggernaut.description: Crimson siege platform. Six weapon mounts. Cracks stations and anything else that holds still.
crimson_juggernaut.lore:
Six weapon mounts. The Juggernaut doesn't chase — it arrives, and
then whatever it arrived at stops existing. Purpose-built for siege
operations against stations, capital ships, and fortified positions,
the Juggernaut trades speed for firepower on a scale that other
empires consider excessive. The Crimson Fleet considers "excessive
firepower" a contradiction in terms. The slow speed is a design
feature: the Juggernaut's role begins after the enemy has been
pinned in place by faster ships. Crimson siege doctrine is simple —
the Juggernaut is the last thing the enemy sees, and it arrives
after they've already lost the ability to run.

nebula_scaffold.name: Scaffold
nebula_scaffold.description: Nebula construction vessel. Builds the infrastructure that generates the revenue.
nebula_scaffold.lore:
The Scaffold-class is the Federation's mid-range construction platform,
deployed wherever a new station, waypoint, or fuel depot needs building.
Unarmed because the Federation would rather spend the tonnage on
building materials than guns. Five utility slots support fabrication
arrays, structural integrity monitors, and project management systems.
Every station the Scaffold builds becomes a new node in the Federation's
trade network — a capital investment with compound returns.

voidborn_sublimation.name: Sublimation
voidborn_sublimation.description: Voidborn refinery ship. Raw materials transform in ways metallurgists cannot replicate.
voidborn_sublimation.lore:
Named for the phase transition from solid directly to gas, the
Sublimation-class refinery processes raw ore through biological
catalysts that other empires cannot synthesize. Metals emerge from the
refining chambers with crystal structures that should require pressures
found only in stellar cores. The Collective's materials scientists
describe the process as "persuading matter to remember what it could
be." Yield rates are consistently above theoretical maximums, which
the Collective attributes to "cooperative thermodynamics."

voidborn_aporia.name: Aporia
voidborn_aporia.description: Voidborn interdictor. Enemies cannot determine whether they are trapped.
voidborn_aporia.lore:
Named for an irresolvable philosophical puzzle, the Aporia does not
prevent escape in any way other empires can identify or counteract.
Ships within its operational radius simply find that their jump
calculations never converge. Navigation systems report ready, then
recalculate, then report ready again. Pilots describe the experience
as "trying to leave a room you can't prove you're in." The Collective
explains the interdiction field as "introducing productive doubt into
deterministic systems."

outerrim_bad_idea.name: Bad Idea
outerrim_bad_idea.description: Outer Rim command ship. Coordinates fleet actions at speeds the fleet can't match.
outerrim_bad_idea.lore:
Every Rim fleet needs someone to coordinate the beautiful chaos, and
the Bad Idea-class provides the processing power and comms arrays to
do it. Named because "forming a Rim fleet" is itself considered a bad
idea by most galactic strategists, it carries enough sensor and CPU
capacity to track dozens of independently-minded Rim captains who each
think they know better. The command staff's primary skill is less
"issuing orders" and more "suggesting things loudly enough to be heard
over the arguments."

voidborn_substrate.name: Substrate
voidborn_substrate.description: Voidborn dimensional processor. Harvests and refines exotic matter from dimensional fractures.
voidborn_substrate.lore:
The Substrate does not process conventional materials. It operates at
the boundary between normal space and the dimensional fractures that
Voidborn technology routinely exploits, extracting what the Collective
calls "null matter" — a substance that exists in a superposition of
states until deliberately collapsed into useful form. The extraction
process requires computational resources that would power a small
station, hence the enormous CPU allocation. Other empires have
attempted to replicate the process. Their attempts produce either
nothing or explosions. The Collective attributes both outcomes to
"insufficient philosophical preparation."

nebula_treasury.name: Treasury
nebula_treasury.description: Nebula luxury yacht. For captains who've earned the right to travel comfortably.
nebula_treasury.lore:
The Treasury-class is the Federation's rolling advertisement for the
rewards of commercial success. Panoramic viewports, climate-controlled
cargo bays (for "delicate acquisitions"), and a passenger suite that
would be excessive in a hotel. The three defense slots protect not just
the hull but the lifestyle investment within. Five utility slots
accommodate everything from entertainment systems to portfolio management
terminals. The Federation offers financing at preferred customer rates.

crimson_vanguard.name: Vanguard
crimson_vanguard.description: Crimson command ship. Leads from the front, not the rear. Fleet coordination through personal example.
crimson_vanguard.lore:
In most navies, the command ship sits behind the battle line. In
the Crimson Fleet, a commander who leads from the rear is a
commander who doesn't lead for long. The Vanguard carries fleet
coordination systems powerful enough to manage a full battle group
while simultaneously engaging targets with its own three weapon
mounts. The extra defense slots exist not to protect the commander —
Crimson commanders consider that cowardice — but to keep the
coordination arrays functioning under fire. A Vanguard that
stops transmitting because it took a hit costs the fleet more
than one captain's pride is worth.

outerrim_frankenhauler.name: Frankenhauler
outerrim_frankenhauler.description: Outer Rim megahauler. Three decommissioned freighters bolted together. Absurd cargo, questionable integrity.
outerrim_frankenhauler.lore:
The Frankenhauler is three decommissioned freighters bolted together
with structural bracing, connected by cargo transfer tunnels, and
fitted with duct-taped jump drives. Eight hundred cargo capacity makes
it the second-largest hauler at T4, behind only the Nebula Conglomerate.
At 75,000 credits it's the cheapest T4 hauler by a wide margin — the
Rim doesn't pay premium for ships that used to be other ships. Speed 2
because the Rim puts engines on everything. Two weapon mounts because
frontier convoys get raided. Hull integrity is "probably fine."
Structural engineers from every other empire agree it shouldn't fly.
It flies anyway.

outerrim_gas_cracker.name: Gas Cracker
outerrim_gas_cracker.description: Outer Rim jury-rigged gas processor. Fast throughput, rough processing, doesn't care about purity.
outerrim_gas_cracker.lore:
The Gas Cracker burns through gas clouds at speed, collection scoops
wide open, processing systems running flat out. Purity standards are
"whatever comes out the other end." The Rim's gas refineries prioritize
throughput over quality — five hundred cargo of roughly-processed gas
is worth more than two hundred cargo of pristine fractions when you're
supplying frontier fuel depots. Speed 2 lets the Gas Cracker follow
gas clouds as they drift, maintaining continuous collection without
the repositioning delays that slow competitors.

outerrim_ice_rig.name: Ice Rig
outerrim_ice_rig.description: Outer Rim improvised ice platform. Welded-together saws, heaters, and cargo pods. Ugly but effective.
outerrim_ice_rig.lore:
The Ice Rig looks exactly like what it is: industrial ice saws,
thermal heaters, and cargo pods welded onto a hull frame with
structural bracing that a Solarian engineer would condemn. It works.
Speed 2 gets it to ice fields before competitors, the saws cut
chunks free fast, the heaters crack them into manageable pieces, and
the cargo pods hold four hundred and fifty units of roughly-processed
ice product. Refinement quality is "adequate." The Rim doesn't have
time for precision cryo-extraction — the frontier needs coolant and
fuel, and the Ice Rig delivers both.

outerrim_rapid_smelter.name: Rapid Smelter
outerrim_rapid_smelter.description: Outer Rim fast smelting platform. High throughput, low finesse. Bolted together but it works.
outerrim_rapid_smelter.lore:
The Rapid Smelter is the Rim's answer to refinery ships: fast, cheap,
and good enough. The smelting systems process basic ores into refined
materials at twice the speed of Solarian precision refineries, at half
the quality. The Rim considers this an acceptable trade-off — volume
beats quality when you're supplying frontier operations that need
materials yesterday. Speed 2 makes it mobile enough to follow mining
operations rather than waiting at a fixed location. The entire smelting
assembly was salvaged from three different decommissioned stations and
welded into a hull that the original engineers would not recognize.

outerrim_good_enough.name: Good Enough
outerrim_good_enough.description: Outer Rim logistics ship. Keeps fleets supplied through sheer determination.
outerrim_good_enough.lore:
Rim logistics is not glamorous work. The Good Enough-class hauls fuel,
ammunition, spare parts, and replacement components to fleet positions
deep in contested or uncharted space. The name captures the Rim's
pragmatic approach to supply chains: the parts don't need to be perfect,
they need to be there. With 500 units of cargo capacity and enough fuel
to reach anywhere the fleet can go, the Good Enough ensures that Rim
operations don't stall for lack of supplies — just for lack of planning.

outerrim_rock_crusher.name: Rock Crusher
outerrim_rock_crusher.description: Outer Rim overbuilt mining platform. Fast for its size, cheap, strips belts bare.
outerrim_rock_crusher.lore:
The Rock Crusher is the only T4 mining platform with speed 2 — the
overclocked engines are technically out of spec, but they work, and
the Rim doesn't believe in specs anyway. Eleven hundred cargo capacity
means it strips belts bare before slower competitors finish their
first load. At 85,000 credits, it's the cheapest T4 miner available
because the Rim builds ships from whatever's available rather than
whatever's optimal. The single weapon mount exists because frontier
mining operations attract attention, and the Rim's response to
attention is always armed.

outerrim_yard_sale.name: Yard Sale
outerrim_yard_sale.description: Outer Rim capital salvager. Three cargo bays, one price: whatever you'll pay.
outerrim_yard_sale.lore:
The Yard Sale is what happens when the Rim needs to clean up after a
capital engagement and none of the core empires are willing to send
their own salvage fleets into disputed space. Three cargo bays — two
purpose-built and one that used to be a Crimson ammunition magazine
before someone removed the explosives and added shelving — hold enough
to strip a capital wreck to the frame. The name reflects Rim philosophy:
everything recovered is for sale, to anyone, immediately. Price
negotiation happens on the flight home. The Yard Sale is faster than
any capital salvager in service, because in the Rim, the second ship to
arrive at a wreck gets whatever the first ship left behind. Which is
usually nothing.

outerrim_manifest_destiny.name: Manifest Destiny
outerrim_manifest_destiny.description: Outer Rim exploration cruiser. The ship that goes where maps end.
outerrim_manifest_destiny.lore:
800 units of fuel. Speed 3 on a large hull. The Manifest Destiny is the
Outer Rim's dedicated deep-space exploration platform, designed for
pilots who believe the galactic edge is where interesting things start,
not where they end. The fuel range enables expeditions that would drain
other ships dry, and the speed ensures a quick return when supplies run
low — or when something at the edge of the map starts following you back.
Every Manifest Destiny that returns brings stories. Not all of them
return.

crimson_strip_mine.name: Strip Mine
crimson_strip_mine.description: Crimson armored mining platform. Fortress hull, can fight back. The Fleet's heavy extraction asset.
crimson_strip_mine.lore:
The Strip Mine-class is what happens when the Crimson Fleet applies
capital ship engineering to asteroid mining. Twelve hundred points of
hull and fifty-five points of armor make it nearly indestructible by
anything short of a dedicated assault force. Two weapon mounts mean
the Strip Mine fights back — mining operations in contested space
continue uninterrupted while the weapons handle interruptions. Eight
hundred cargo capacity fills over sustained extraction operations that
other empires would consider a siege. The Fleet considers the Strip
Mine a strategic asset: it doesn't just mine a system, it claims it.

voidborn_thermodynamic_end.name: Thermodynamic End
voidborn_thermodynamic_end.description: Voidborn capital salvager. Envelops entire capital wrecks and reduces them to sorted material streams.
voidborn_thermodynamic_end.lore:
The Thermodynamic End is the Collective's capital salvage vessel, named
for the principle it embodies: all ordered structures tend toward
dissolution, and the Collective merely accelerates the process. Its
organic hull can envelop an entire capital wreck, breaking it down into
sorted material streams over the course of hours. The recovered materials
emerge at a purity that exceeds the original manufacturing specification
— a result that Solarian metallurgists have called "thermodynamically
implausible." The Collective finds the objection quaint.

solarian_archive.name: Archive
solarian_archive.description: Solarian capital salvager. Full materials science laboratory and capital-scale wreck recovery.
solarian_archive.lore:
The Archive-class represents the Confederacy's conviction that no wreck
is merely debris — it is data waiting to be preserved. This capital-scale
salvage platform carries a full materials science laboratory, redundant
scanning arrays, and enough cargo capacity to recover the remains of a
capital engagement single-handed. Every component recovered is assigned
a unique tracking number that follows it through the Confederacy's supply
chain indefinitely. Solarian engineers maintain that this is simply good
practice. Everyone else maintains that it is excessive. Both are correct.

crimson_cauterizer.name: Cauterizer
crimson_cauterizer.description: Crimson combat hospital. Patches crews and sends them back into the fight. Triage, not therapy.
crimson_cauterizer.lore:
The Cauterizer's name tells you everything about Crimson medical
philosophy: close the wound, stop the bleeding, get them back in
the fight. The five utility slots accommodate medical bays, trauma
systems, and pharmaceutical fabricators optimized for rapid
treatment rather than comprehensive care. Crimson combat medics
train in triage under fire — literally, since the Cauterizer
operates in active combat zones. Two weapon mounts are "standard
Crimson medical equipment" according to fleet procurement. Three
defense slots keep the hospital functional. The cargo hold carries
medical supplies and replacement crew — because the Crimson Fleet
treats both as combat consumables.

crimson_gas_cracker.name: Gas Cracker
crimson_gas_cracker.description: Crimson weaponized gas processor. Refines volatiles into fuel, explosives, and chemical weapons components.
crimson_gas_cracker.lore:
The Gas Cracker-class refines volatile gases into three categories
the Crimson Fleet cares about: fuel for the fleet, explosives for
ordnance, and chemical components for weapons systems. The refinery
processes run at temperatures and pressures that violate every other
empire's safety standards, which the Fleet considers a competitive
advantage. Two weapon mounts protect gas processing operations in
contested nebulae — the Fleet learned that losing a full cargo of
refined volatiles to a pirate raid is more expensive than the guns.

crimson_guillotine.name: Guillotine
crimson_guillotine.description: Crimson capital salvage platform. Follows battle groups at a respectful distance and reduces what remains to raw materials.
crimson_guillotine.lore:
The Guillotine is the Crimson Fleet's capital salvage platform, and
calling it a salvage ship undersells it considerably. Four weapon mounts
ensure the wreck field stays clear of competitors while industrial cutting
arrays reduce capital-class hulls to raw materials with mechanical
indifference. The vessel's name was chosen by committee vote among the
inaugural crew. The runner-up was "Carrion." The Guillotine follows
Crimson battle groups at a respectful distance, waiting for the
engagement to conclude. The fleet considers this patience. Everyone else
considers it ominous.

crimson_ice_fortress.name: Ice Fortress
crimson_ice_fortress.description: Crimson thermal lance ice platform. Brute-force cracking with military-grade systems.
crimson_ice_fortress.lore:
The Ice Fortress-class applies military-grade thermal systems to ice
extraction and processing at capital scale. The thermal lances operate
at temperatures that liquify ice formations in seconds, and the
refinery systems separate the resulting melt into fuel components,
coolant, and rare crystalline materials. The Fleet needs coolant for
weapon systems, fuel for fleet operations, and the Ice Fortress
produces both in quantities that keep a battle group operational
indefinitely. The hull armor doubles as thermal shielding — the
extraction systems generate enough waste heat to damage nearby ships.

nebula_liquidator.name: Liquidator
nebula_liquidator.description: Nebula heavy salvager. Industrial-scale asset recovery from capital wrecks.
nebula_liquidator.lore:
The Liquidator-class is the Federation's heavy salvage platform,
designed to strip capital-class wrecks down to their component atoms
and haul the results to market. 500 units of cargo capacity and
industrial cutting beams make short work of even the largest derelicts.
The Federation's salvage division motto: "The market finds a use for
everything." The Liquidator is how they prove it.

solarian_logistics_prime.name: Logistics Prime
solarian_logistics_prime.description: Solarian automated logistics platform. Self-organizing cargo bays and optimal packing algorithms.
solarian_logistics_prime.lore:
The Logistics Prime-class applies the Confederacy's optimization
expertise to cargo transport at capital scale. Self-organizing cargo
bays use automated systems to pack, track, and rearrange cargo for
optimal mass distribution — the ship literally rearranges its own
cargo hold in transit to maintain perfect trim. Seven hundred cargo
capacity is achieved through efficiency rather than brute volume.
The CPU runs logistics optimization that calculates multi-stop routes,
dynamic pricing, and load balancing in real time. The Confederacy
considers this the minimum standard for professional logistics.

nebula_conglomerate.name: Conglomerate
nebula_conglomerate.description: Nebula mega-hauler. The largest non-capital cargo vessel in production.
nebula_conglomerate.lore:
600 units of cargo capacity. The Conglomerate-class is the Federation's
ultimate commercial vessel — a mobile warehouse that makes inter-empire
trade routes profitable at scale. Slow, but speed is less important
when each trip generates enough revenue to fund a small fleet. The
five utility slots support logistics management, automated inventory
systems, and the Federation's "Total Commercial Awareness" suite.
Insurance premiums for a fully loaded Conglomerate have their own
financial instrument category.

solarian_deep_core_platform.name: Deep Core Platform
solarian_deep_core_platform.description: Solarian precision mining capital. The most methodical extraction system in the galaxy.
solarian_deep_core_platform.lore:
The Deep Core Platform represents the Confederacy's conviction that
mining is applied geology at industrial scale. Eight utility slots
accommodate the most comprehensive geological analysis suite ever
mounted on a mining vessel — real-time seismic modeling, spectral
tomography, and core sample analysis that maps every ore deposit in
an asteroid before the first laser fires. The result is extraction
yields that approach theoretical maximums. One thousand cargo capacity
holds the results of operations that take longer but waste nothing.
Solarian mining doctrine: the richest ore is the ore you don't leave
behind.

solarian_encyclopedia.name: Encyclopedia
solarian_encyclopedia.description: Solarian logistics ship. Supplies and supports entire fleet operations.
solarian_encyclopedia.lore:
The Encyclopedia-class carries everything a fleet needs and the
documentation to prove it was packed correctly. The massive cargo bays
support extended fleet operations, while the six utility slots accommodate
repair, resupply, and coordination systems. Solarian logistics officers
consider an Encyclopedia's inventory manifest a work of art — every item
cross-referenced, every consumable tracked, every spare part catalogued
with its maintenance history.

crimson_war_wagon.name: War Wagon
crimson_war_wagon.description: Crimson freight battleship. Carries cargo through hostile space by force. More guns than most warships.
crimson_war_wagon.lore:
The War Wagon-class is the Crimson Fleet's answer to the question
"what if a freighter could win a fight?" Three weapon mounts and
fifty-five points of armor make it better armed and armored than most
dedicated warships at this tier. Five hundred cargo capacity is less
than civilian haulers, but the War Wagon doesn't need escorts —
it IS the escort. Crimson merchants learned centuries ago that the
cheapest shipping insurance is a hull that shoots back. The War Wagon
doesn't avoid hostile space. It goes through hostile space and dares
anything to try stopping it.

solarian_cryo_laboratory.name: Cryo Laboratory
solarian_cryo_laboratory.description: Solarian ice research platform. Precision cryo-extraction identifies compounds others destroy.
solarian_cryo_laboratory.lore:
The Cryo Laboratory-class treats ice extraction as materials science.
Thermal regulators maintain extraction zones at precisely controlled
temperatures, allowing crystallographic analysis to identify and
isolate rare compounds that brute-force ice breaking destroys. The
seven utility slots accommodate instruments that other empires would
mount in a planetary laboratory — the Confederacy considers this the
minimum standard for serious cryo-extraction work. Processing is
slower than competitors but yields compounds that other ships cannot
produce at all.

voidborn_dimensional_fold.name: Dimensional Fold
voidborn_dimensional_fold.description: Voidborn phase-shifted hauler. Cargo exists in compressed dimensional pockets. Extreme capacity.
voidborn_dimensional_fold.lore:
The Dimensional Fold-class applies Voidborn compression technology to
commercial hauling at capital scale. Cargo bay volume is multiplied
by partial phase-shifting into compressed dimensional pockets — the
ship's internal volume exceeds its external dimensions by a factor
that other empires' physicists refuse to acknowledge. Six hundred and
fifty cargo capacity in a hull that external measurements suggest
should hold 300. The dimensional fold requires constant shield power
to maintain stability, explaining the nine hundred shield points.
Voidborn quartermasters consider dimensional logistics normal.
Everyone else considers it deeply unsettling.

solarian_fractional_distillery.name: Fractional Distillery
solarian_fractional_distillery.description: Solarian precision gas refinery. Fractional distillation with scientific accuracy. Best yield ratios.
solarian_fractional_distillery.lore:
The Fractional Distillery-class separates gas mixtures with laboratory-
grade precision at industrial scale. Seven utility slots support
spectrometers, composition analyzers, and fractional separation columns
that sort gas streams by molecular weight, reactivity, and commercial
value simultaneously. The Confederacy's gas processing doctrine
maximizes value extraction from every cubic meter of collected gas —
compounds that other empires vent as waste, the Distillery isolates
and stores as premium products. Processing quality is the highest in
the galaxy because the Confederacy considers anything less to be
unprofessional.

voidborn_resonance_array.name: Resonance Array
voidborn_resonance_array.description: Voidborn resonance excavation platform. Vibrates ore free through dimensional harmonics at capital scale.
voidborn_resonance_array.lore:
The Resonance Array-class extends Voidborn harmonic extraction to
capital scale. The dimensional frequency generators vibrate entire
asteroid fields at their resonant frequency, separating ore from rock
across multiple targets simultaneously. Nine hundred shield points
protect against resonance feedback — when you vibrate asteroids at
frequencies that shouldn't exist, the universe occasionally objects.
Eight utility slots accommodate the sensor arrays, harmonic tuners,
and dimensional stabilizers needed for safe operation. The Collective
insists the process is "cooperative" rather than "destructive." The
asteroids have not been asked for comment.

crimson_arsenal.name: Arsenal
crimson_arsenal.description: Crimson fleet logistics. Ammunition, fuel, replacement weapons. The reason the Fleet keeps fighting.
crimson_arsenal.lore:
Every weapon in the Crimson Fleet fires ammunition that comes
from somewhere. That somewhere is the Arsenal. Five hundred units
of cargo capacity carry weapons, ammunition, fuel, replacement
armor plates, and everything else a battle group consumes in
sustained operations. The Arsenal is the most heavily armored
logistics vessel in any navy — because the Crimson Fleet learned
the hard way that losing your supply ship loses the campaign.
Arsenal captains hold the least glamorous but most critical
position in the fleet. A Crimson battle group without an Arsenal
is a Crimson battle group with a countdown timer.

solarian_crucible.name: Crucible
solarian_crucible.description: Solarian refinery ship. Mobile ore processing with laboratory-grade purity standards.
solarian_crucible.lore:
The Crucible-class produces refined materials that exceed Confederacy purity
standards — which are already the strictest in known space. The mobile
refinery processes raw ore through seven sequential purification stages,
each monitored by independent quality-control sensors. Output materials
are stamped with unique batch identifiers and accompanied by a certificate
of analysis. Rejected batches (anything below 99.97% purity) are recycled
automatically. The waste heat recovery system was nominated for three
engineering awards.

voidborn_cryo_phaser.name: Cryo Phaser
voidborn_cryo_phaser.description: Voidborn anomalous ice harvester. Phase-shifts through ice to extract materials from deep cores.
voidborn_cryo_phaser.lore:
The Cryo Phaser-class harvests ice through dimensional phase-shifting
at capital scale. The extraction system reaches through the surface of
ice bodies without disturbing them, pulling target materials directly
from deep cores that conventional equipment cannot access. Nine hundred
shield points protect against the dimensional stress of sustained
phase-extraction operations. The technique is unique to the Voidborn —
other empires lack the dimensional technology to reach into solid
matter without breaking it. The Collective considers this approach
both more efficient and more respectful of the ice body's structural
integrity.

outerrim_excessive_force.name: Excessive Force
outerrim_excessive_force.description: Outer Rim heavy drone carrier. Deploys swarms of mismatched drones with alarming enthusiasm.
outerrim_excessive_force.lore:
The Excessive Force-class carries enough drone bays to darken a sensor
screen. Rim drone warfare philosophy scales up the same way Rim
engineering scales up everything: more of it, faster, with less regard
for matching specifications. The drone complement is assembled from
whatever was available at the last three ports — attack drones, mining
drones, reconnaissance drones, and a few that the crew can't positively
identify but which seem aggressive. The name is what opposing captains
file in their after-action reports.

nebula_mega_hauler.name: Mega Hauler
nebula_mega_hauler.description: Nebula mega ore hauler. Obscene cargo capacity. Empties asteroid belts and hauls everything to market.
nebula_mega_hauler.lore:
The Mega Hauler-class exists because the Federation asked "what if we
just... carried more?" Fourteen hundred cargo capacity is achieved
through structural engineering that other empires consider irresponsible
— the cargo bay occupies over 60% of the hull volume. Zero weapon
mounts because the Federation considers guns on a mining platform a
waste of cargo space. Escorts exist for a reason, and ore in the hold
generates revenue that guns never will. The Mega Hauler doesn't mine
efficiently — it mines thoroughly, stripping asteroid belts down to
pebbles and hauling everything to market in a single trip.

nebula_paramount.name: Paramount
nebula_paramount.description: Nebula command cruiser. Coordinates trade convoys and fleet operations.
nebula_paramount.lore:
The Paramount-class serves as the nerve center for Federation trade
convoys and the occasional military operation. The massive CPU array
runs route optimization, market analysis, and fleet coordination
simultaneously. Five utility slots support every conceivable support
system, and the generous cargo hold ensures the Paramount generates
revenue even while commanding. The Federation considers profit and
military effectiveness complementary, not contradictory.

voidborn_phase_condenser.name: Phase Condenser
voidborn_phase_condenser.description: Voidborn exotic gas processor. Extracts gases from dimensional rifts that other ships can't detect.
voidborn_phase_condenser.lore:
The Phase Condenser-class extracts gases from dimensional rifts that
other empires' sensors cannot detect. The collection membranes extend
into adjacent dimensional layers, capturing exotic compounds that
exist only at the boundary between realities. The condensation process
forces these inter-dimensional gases into normal-space stable states,
producing materials with properties that conventional chemistry cannot
explain. Seven utility slots support the phase-detection and
stabilization equipment needed to operate safely — "safely" being
a relative term when harvesting matter from between dimensions.

voidborn_alloy_synthesizer.name: Alloy Synthesizer
voidborn_alloy_synthesizer.description: Voidborn dimensional alloy forge. Combines multiple ore types into advanced alloys in a single step.
voidborn_alloy_synthesizer.lore:
The Alloy Synthesizer-class combines multiple raw ore types into
advanced alloys through dimensional compression that skips intermediate
refining stages entirely. Feed it iron, titanium, and null matter, and
the synthesis chambers produce alloys with crystal structures that
should require planetary-scale foundries. The Collective's materials
scientists describe the process as "showing matter the shape it wants
to take." Processing cycles are slow — each synthesis requires
dimensional stabilization that takes time — but a single step replaces
three conventional refining stages. Other empires cannot replicate
the process because it requires dimensional phase states they cannot
generate.

nebula_gas_compressor.name: Gas Compressor
nebula_gas_compressor.description: Nebula bulk gas collection and compression vessel. Enormous intake, stores compressed gas for transport.
nebula_gas_compressor.lore:
The Gas Compressor-class was designed by the same Federation accounting
department that produces all their best ships. Enormous intake scoops
channel gas into compression tanks that store collected gas at densities
other empires' safety regulators would reject. Six hundred and fifty
cargo capacity carries both raw and compressed gas — the Federation
hauls everything and lets the station-side brokers sort out pricing.
Unarmed because guns don't generate revenue. The Gas Compressor doesn't
care about purity or processing — it cares about volume, and it has
more of it than anyone.

nebula_ice_logistics.name: Ice Logistics
nebula_ice_logistics.description: Nebula industrial ice harvester-hauler. Carves entire ice asteroids and hauls them whole.
nebula_ice_logistics.lore:
The Ice Logistics-class approaches frozen resources the way the
Federation approaches everything: as a supply chain problem. The
ship's tractor systems carve entire ice bodies free from asteroid
fields and secure them in the cavernous cargo bay for transport to
processing facilities. Why refine in the field when station-side
facilities do it better? Six hundred cargo capacity carries ice
in bulk — whole chunks, minimally processed, maximum volume. The
cryo-containment systems maintain cargo at extraction temperature,
preventing melt loss that the Federation considers an unacceptable
hit to the profit margin.

solarian_panacea.name: Panacea
solarian_panacea.description: Solarian medical cruiser. Full hospital capabilities with research-grade facilities.
solarian_panacea.lore:
The Panacea-class is a fully accredited hospital ship with research
capabilities that rival most planetary medical centers. Four independent
life support units maintain separate sterile environments for surgical,
recovery, quarantine, and research operations. The medical AI holds
honorary degrees from three Solarian universities and maintains a
diagnostic accuracy rate the Confederacy Medical Board describes as
"statistically indistinguishable from perfect." Patients receive a
comprehensive discharge summary whether they want one or not.

solarian_axiomata.name: Axiomata
solarian_axiomata.description: Solarian battlecruiser. The Confederacy's commitment to measured force.
solarian_axiomata.lore:
The Axiomata-class battlecruiser represents the Confederacy's belief that
